TIME, Buzzfeed, The Wall Street Journal, and a host of other prestigious publications have lauded "Number One Chinese Restaurant" as a must-read. Critics rave, with Buzzfeed calling it "a joy to read—I couldn't get enough," and Chang-rae Lee, a New York Times bestselling author, describing it as a novel that "practically thumps with heartache and sharp humor."

This exuberant and wise multigenerational debut novel delves into the intricate lives and loves of those who make their living within the walls of a beloved Chinese restaurant. The Beijing Duck House in Rockville, Maryland, is more than just a popular spot for satisfying cravings and marking celebrations; it's a self-contained universe. For decades, waiters and kitchen staff have navigated its confines, experiencing the full spectrum of human emotion – fighting, loving, and growing older together.

### The Heart of the House

When an unforeseen disaster shatters the restaurant's carefully managed chaos, each character is compelled to confront the personal conflicts that the relentless pace of restaurant life had previously held at bay.

#### Shifting Tides for the Han Family

Owner Jimmy Han harbors a desire to elevate his late father's humble establishment into something more sophisticated. Meanwhile, Jimmy's older brother, Johnny, and Johnny's daughter, Annie, yearn to recapture a past fractured by a father's absence and a teenager's withdrawal.

#### Enduring Bonds and New Beginnings

Longtime Duck House employees, Nan and Ah-Jack, find themselves contemplating a deepening of their thirty-year friendship. This introspection occurs even as Nan's son, Pat, grapples with staying on the right side of trouble.

#### A Dangerous Game

The narrative takes a perilous turn when Pat and Annie, entangled by youthful desire and a shared sense of ennui, become implicated in the tragedy that befalls the Duck House. This dangerous entanglement forces their families to confront the extent of their willingness to sacrifice for their children.

### A Tapestry of Relationships

"Number One Chinese Restaurant" is a novel rich in spirit and intelligence. Its multi-voiced narrative offers a poignant and darkly humorous exploration of the complexities of family. Moving beyond the superficial trappings of red tablecloths and silkscreen murals, it unveils an unforgettable story about the intertwined experiences of youth and aging, parents and children, and the profound ways in which families can both wound us and serve as our anchors, keeping us grounded and alive.

Jacqueline Woodson's novel, *Red at the Bone*, offers a profound exploration of how family, identity, and inherited legacies shape our lives. Set in Brooklyn in 2001, the story focuses on sixteen-year-old Melody as she anticipates a significant coming-of-age ceremony. This event, marked by a special dress and the music of Prince, becomes a catalyst, revealing the intertwined histories of two families from different social backgrounds.

Woodson's narrative delves into themes of sexual desire, the complexities of forming an identity, and the deep influence of parenthood. The novel skillfully connects Melody's family history to crucial moments in American history, such as the 1921 Tulsa race massacre and the post-9/11 period in New York City. Through these links, Woodson shows how young people often face life-changing decisions before they fully understand themselves or their future goals.

*Red at the Bone* has been widely praised by critics, appearing on "Best Of" lists from The New York Times, The Washington Post, Time, and NPR. Praised for its "sublime" writing and "generous and big-hearted" storytelling, the novel highlights Woodson's talent for depicting changing family relationships and the strength of human connections. This "little masterpiece" provides a moving and insightful look at the pleasure and understanding that literature can bring.

Name: Something Strange and Deadly
Description:
Sixteen-year-old Eleanor Fitt’s brother is missing. And when she discovers that the Dead are rising in Philadelphia and wreaking havoc throughout the city, she knows that her brother is involved. So Eleanor enlists the help of the Spirit-Hunters. This motley crew, hired to protect the city from supernatural forces, is after the necromancer who has been reanimating corpses. Their skills can save her brother. But as Eleanor spends time with the Spirit-Hunters, and their handsome inventor, Daniel, the situation becomes dire. Now not only is her reputation at risk, but her very life may hang in the balance. In Something Strange and Deadly, the first book in a trilogy, Susan Dennard weaves together vividly imagined scenes of action, adventure, and gorgeous Victorian fashion to create an entertaining steampunk tapestry of humor, horror, and romance. Readers who love Cassandra Clare’s Infernal Devices series will be intrigued from the start.

## Murder at Half Moon Gate: A Regency Mystery of Invention and Intrigue

In the heart of Regency London, where appearances are often deceiving, the Earl of Wrexford and the sharp-witted Charlotte Sloane unite to unravel a perplexing murder. The investigation begins when Lord Wrexford stumbles upon the lifeless body of a brilliant inventor in a shadowy London alley. Initially, he entrusts the matter to the authorities, alerting the watchman.

### A Plea for Help and Missing Designs

However, Wrexford finds himself increasingly entangled in the case when the inventor's grieving widow implores him for assistance. Her husband's groundbreaking designs for a revolutionary steam-powered engine have vanished on the very night of his demise. These plans hold immense potential value, capable of generating a fortune, but also pose a significant threat if they fall into the wrong hands.

### An Unlikely Partnership

Joining Wrexford in his pursuit of the truth is Charlotte Sloane. Under the pseudonym A. J. Quill, Sloane is renowned for her incisive political cartoons. She readily leverages her extensive network of informants, crucial to her journalistic endeavors, to track down the elusive killer.

### Unraveling the Motives

As peril escalates with each step, the formidable partnership of Wrexford's keen analytical mind and Sloane's astute intuition begins to expose the complex motivations behind the inventor's tragic end. They are in a race against time, confronting a killer prepared to strike again before the inventor's invaluable designs can be recovered.

### Critical Acclaim

*   "Penrose deftly combines a Regency romance with a tricky mystery that delves into social unrest and the darker side of this storied period." —Kirkus Reviews
*   "Its complex story line and authentic historical details bring the early days of the Industrial Revolution vividly to life. Bound to fascinate readers of C.S. Harris and even fans of Victorian mysteries." —Library Journal, Starred Review

## Leonora Bolt: Secret Inventor - A STEM Adventure

Dive into a world of "fabulous inventions," "friendship," and "fun" with Leonora Bolt, a young girl who is far from ordinary. Leonora is a brilliant inventor, dedicating her days to a TOP SECRET laboratory where she meticulously designs and builds incredible contraptions. Her uncle, a rather imposing figure, supervises her creative pursuits. However, Leonora's carefully constructed world is dramatically disrupted by the unexpected arrival of a mysterious boy riding an inflatable lobster. This encounter leads to a startling revelation: her uncle has been secretly stealing her inventions and selling them off the island.

This shocking discovery compels Leonora to leave her secluded life on Crabby Island for the first time. Equipped with her most ingenious creations, she embarks on a remarkable journey that will test her intellect to its very limits. Her quest introduces her to a colorful array of characters, including an otter with an extraordinary skill, a delightfully eccentric cook, and a sea captain with a passion for singing. The central question remains: can Leonora leverage her exceptional mind to invent a solution that will finally outwit her unscrupulous uncle and reclaim her stolen work? This STEM-focused adventure, echoing the spirit of characters like Violet Baudelaire and Peanut Jones, is a vibrant tribute to self-confidence and the boundless potential of individual ingenuity.

## Unveiling Iduna and Agnarr's Past: Secrets of the Enchanted Forest

The novel *Frozen 2: Dangerous Secrets* offers a captivating glimpse into the formative years of Queen Iduna and King Agnarr, revealing the hidden truths that predated the events of *Frozen 2*. The narrative introduces a sixteen-year-old Iduna, an ambitious Arendellian inventor with a profound dual identity.

Iduna navigates her life as a trusted confidante to Prince Agnarr, all while concealing her Northuldra heritage. This secret is a dangerous one, given Arendelle's deep-seated animosity towards the Northuldra people. This animosity stems from the enigmatic "day the forest fell," an event that fractured relations and led to the Northuldra, along with some Arendellians, being isolated within the Enchanted Forest, separated by an impenetrable mist. Iduna's primary concern is her own safety, necessitating the concealment of her true origins.

Meanwhile, Prince Agnarr harbors fragmented memories of a Northuldra girl from the chaotic day he lost his father and narrowly escaped death. Despite these hazy recollections, Agnarr values Iduna as a loyal ally as he prepares to assume the Arendellian throne, navigating royal duties, a formidable council, and the guidance of a regent. As their bond deepens, Iduna and Agnarr's friendship blossoms into a romance. This burgeoning affection, however, poses significant challenges, threatening Iduna's precarious position and Agnarr's royal aspirations. The immense risks involved force them to confront the profound dangers and sacrifices inherent in their love, underscoring the enduring power of concealed truths.

Name: The Inventor's Secret
Description:
When twins Abby and Derick start junior high at the prestigious academy their grandfather founded, Cragbridge Hall, they discover firsthand the dangers of time travel and must find a way to save their parents, who have been sent to the Titanic the night it sank.

**LEO, Inventor Extraordinaire: A Middle Grade Mystery of Identity and Invention**

For puzzle-loving middle schoolers, *LEO, Inventor Extraordinaire* offers a thrilling adventure. Leo, a dedicated student at the secluded Academy of Florence, embarks on a quest to uncover his family's hidden history and the truth behind his orphaned status. His journey is fraught with challenges, requiring him to rely on his inventive genius, even when his creations don't quite cooperate.

Beneath the familiar halls of his school, Leo stumbles upon a hidden network of tunnels. These subterranean passages are not merely a maze; they are a carefully constructed series of clues, riddles, and puzzles designed to reveal his identity and his family's secrets. As a "lifer" at the academy, Leo has never known his parents or any relatives. His closest companions are his mechanical monkey, his robot lion, and his loyal best friend, Savvy. Together, this unlikely trio often finds themselves in predicatments.

Leo's latest experiment takes an unexpected and disastrous turn, leading him to a cryptic clue. This discovery unlocks an underground labyrinth, seemingly crafted specifically for him to navigate. Leo believes these tunnels hold the answers to his parents' identities and the reason for his orphanhood. However, his investigation reveals a deeper connection between his past, his future, and the innovative Wynn Toys company. The company's brilliant president vanished years ago under mysterious circumstances.

To unravel the truth, Leo must harness his creativity and scientific acumen. He needs to revitalize the struggling toy company, confront its villainous current leader, and ultimately discover the fate of his own family.

*LEO, Inventor Extraordinaire* is an ideal read for children aged 8 to 12 who crave action, adventure, humor, and mystery. It's often compared to *The Da Vinci Code* for kids, featuring robots, imaginative inventions, and engaging puzzles. The book is enhanced by nearly 60 pieces of original black and white artwork, fostering imagination and promoting STEAM skills. This captivating novel was recognized with the Northern Lights Book Award for Middle Grade Fiction in 2021.

Name: Who Killed Darius Drake?: A Mystery
Description:
From Newbery Honor author Rodman Philbrick comes a delicious, fast-paced whodunit about two socially outcast boys who set out to find jewels hidden for a century--and unwittingly flush out a murderer. In award-winner Philbrick's new page-turning mystery, Arthur "Bash Man" is the school thug, paid with candy to bully and threaten other students. When genius orphan Darius Drake employs Arthur to help him discover the origin of a suspicious threat, written in blood, they uncover a mystery that involves Darius's estranged grandfather, who was imprisoned for forging evidence in a search for a long-lost diamond necklace worth millions. The boys make the dangerous decision to search for the jewels themselves -- and in the process, they discover that the car crash that killed Darius's parents was not an accident at all. Who will be next?Where are the diamonds? And who is stalking the boys? In his first mystery for children, Philbrick delivers yet another suspenseful, unpredictable tale filled with twists and turns that will leave readers breathless.

Name: Deadly Secrets
Description:
Gavin’s life will be turned upside down when he joins a company to work on a product designed to revolutionise the food industry. His initial gut instinct is to walk away, then he discovers one of the company directors is the former love of his teenage life. The financial implications are global and incredible. Powerful individuals and countries are prepared to kill as they compete to seize control of the company. Corruption at high levels, a deadly flaw in the product, and the stakes jump higher and higher. Against overwhelming odds, Gavin must rescue his former love from the hands of an evil cult as they prepare her for a living nightmare.

## Understanding Critical Incidents in Journalism

This collection delves into significant events that have profoundly impacted journalists and their work across various media landscapes. It explores how journalists and other involved parties navigate the complexities of their profession when faced with these pivotal moments.

### Defining Critical Incidents

A critical incident is defined as an event that compels journalists to re-evaluate their established routines, their understanding of their roles, and the rules that govern their practice. These incidents can range from major global events, such as the Rwandan genocide, to specific scandals, like the News of the World hacking affair in the UK.

### Scope and Analysis

The book offers a blend of theoretical and practical insights. It examines the key types of events journalists cover, including political upheaval and natural disasters, as well as incidents that directly affect journalists themselves. Through case studies from diverse countries like Australia, Germany, Brazil, Kenya, and the Philippines, the collection investigates the conversations and debates sparked by these critical events. It analyzes how journalists and other stakeholders have reacted and how these events have subsequently reshaped, or are continuing to reshape, journalistic norms and practices.

### A Roadmap for Future Study

Furthermore, this valuable resource provides a framework for future research into these crucial junctures in journalism. It serves as an essential guide for students and academics in journalism studies, encompassing areas such as journalism history, the sociology of news, digital journalism, and political communication.

The Data Journalism Handbook unlocks the exciting potential of combining digital information with journalistic skills. It explores how a journalist's instinct for a story, coupled with the vast amount of available digital data, creates new avenues for reporting. This handbook delves into the possibilities, boundaries, and practical applications within this dynamic field.

Launched as a collaborative project by the European Journalism Centre and the Open Knowledge Foundation at MozFest in 2011, the handbook has gathered insights from numerous experts. These contributors include prominent journalists, academics, software developers, and data analysts. They share their expertise on how data can serve as the foundation for a story or as a powerful tool to enhance its telling, and sometimes, both.

Readers will discover how organizations like the BBC, Chicago Tribune, and The Guardian utilize data journalism. The handbook features detailed case studies examining its application in areas such as elections, public unrest, school performance, and uncovering corruption. It also provides practical guidance on acquiring data from the web, leveraging freedom of information requests, and employing crowd-sourcing techniques. Furthermore, you'll learn essential methods for extracting meaningful insights from raw data, including working with numbers and statistics, and effectively using data visualization. The handbook concludes by illustrating various ways to deliver data-driven stories, such as through infographics, interactive news apps, open data platforms, and downloadable datasets.

## Understanding the Global Journalist in the 21st Century

This comprehensive volume, *The Global Journalist in the 21st Century*, offers a deep dive into the lives and work of journalists across the globe. It systematically examines the demographics, educational backgrounds, socialization processes, professional attitudes, and working conditions of media professionals in a wide array of countries.

### Building on a Foundation

This updated edition significantly expands upon the original *Global Journalist* published in 1998. It incorporates new data and includes insights from over a dozen additional nations, providing a richer, more current perspective. The editors have curated this collection with a core assumption: that a journalist's background, their daily working environment, and their personal beliefs directly influence the news that is reported and the way it is covered. This influence, they argue, persists even when faced with societal and organizational pressures, and ultimately shapes global public opinion and policy.

### Key Features of the Study

*The Global Journalist in the 21st Century* stands out for several key features:

*   **Global Reach:** The research encompasses 33 nations from diverse regions worldwide, drawing on recent surveys of representative samples of local journalists.
*   **Expert Analysis:** Each country's section features comprehensive analyses penned by renowned media scholars with deep expertise in their respective national contexts.
*   **Comparative Insights:** A dedicated section explores comparative studies of journalists, offering valuable frameworks for understanding cross-national trends.
*   **Methodological Appendix:** An appendix provides a valuable collection of survey questions utilized in various nations to gather data from journalists, offering a practical resource for future research.

### A Definitive Resource

As the most extensive and authoritative source available on journalists worldwide, *The Global Journalist* is poised to become the go-to reference for evaluating the current state of the profession. According to the editors, this book is set to become a standard text for students and researchers in journalism, media studies, and communication programs globally. This research is particularly relevant for those interested in conducting their own comparative studies on journalists.

Name: Behind the Headlines: A Seasoned Journalist's Journey
Description:
In "Behind the Headlines: A Seasoned Journalist's Journey," Mark Dash shares his captivating experiences as a seasoned journalist navigating the complex world of news and storytelling. From his beginnings as a communication student to his adventures as a former poker player turned writer, Dash delves into the challenges and triumphs of his career. This insightful memoir explores the art of crafting trustworthy content while uncovering the intersection of online gambling, travel, and entertainment in New Zealand. Through engaging anecdotes and valuable lessons, readers will discover the passion, integrity, and resilience that define a life dedicated to journalism.

SuperMedia offers a vibrant and opinionated exploration of liberal journalism's vital role in a thriving society. This compelling text delves into the profound transformations currently reshaping the journalistic landscape, driven by social, economic, and technological forces. It critically examines how these shifts are paving the way for an entirely new paradigm of journalism.

The book investigates the significant impact of social networking sites and blogs on established media outlets. Furthermore, it makes a bold and often controversial argument: that journalism itself can serve as a powerful catalyst for addressing and resolving many of the world's most pressing challenges. Authored by a distinguished broadcast journalist, SuperMedia provides a practical and insightful roadmap. It guides readers in identifying key issues and potential solutions necessary to safeguard an open and dependable news media for future generations.

Lewis Raven Wallace's "The View from Somewhere" challenges the traditional journalistic ideal of objectivity, arguing it can hinder truth-seeking and silence important voices. The book explores how the concept of "objectivity" in journalism has evolved, often serving to control narratives and exclude marginalized perspectives, a pattern with roots in historical figures like Ida B. Wells.

Wallace advocates for journalists dedicated to truth and transparency, even when facing opposition from powerful entities and their own industry. He highlights individuals who thoughtfully engage with the idea of "objectivity."

The book showcases journalists who question standard notions of objectivity, such as Desmond Cole, Linda Greenhouse, Rachel Kaadzi Ghansah, John Biewen, Gary Younge, and Meredith Talusan. These journalists, among others, have bravely pushed back against established norms.

Drawing from his own experiences as a transgender journalist and activist from the Midwest, Wallace shares how he was removed from a national public radio reporting position after criticizing coverage of Trump and white supremacy, specifically questioning the prevailing "objectivity" standards.

Through historical analysis and modern examples, Wallace argues that journalists have never been truly neutral. He connects this critique from the violence of 19th-century lynchings to current debates on transgender issues. Wallace contends that "objectivity" often presents a shallow imitation of real reporting, advocating for the rejection of this harmful myth to better address institutional power, racism, and other oppressions within the news media. Ultimately, "The View from Somewhere" calls for a move toward genuine storytelling, stressing the value of news delivered through personal and authentic viewpoints.

Name: Reinventing Professionalism
Description:
Current anxiety about the future of news makes it opportune to revisit the notion of professionalism in journalism. Media expert Silvio Waisbord takes this pressing issue as his theme and argues that “professional journalism” is both a normative and analytical notion. It refers to reporting that observes certain ethical standards as well as to collective efforts by journalists to exercise control over the news. Professionalism should not be narrowly associated with the normative ideal as it historically developed in the West during the past century. Instead, it needs to be approached as a valuable concept to throw into sharp relief how journalists define conditions and rules of work within certain settings. Professionalization is about the specialization of labor and control of occupational practice. These issues are important, particularly amidst the combination of political, technological and economic trends that have profoundly unsettled the foundations of modern journalism. By doing so, they have stimulated the reinvention of professionalism. This engaging and insightful book critically examines the meanings, expectations, and critiques of professional journalism in a global context.

Name: Worlds of Journalism
Description:
How do journalists around the world view their roles and responsibilities in society? Based on a landmark study that has collected data from more than 27,500 journalists in 67 countries, Worlds of Journalism offers a groundbreaking analysis of the different ways journalists perceive their duties, their relationship to society and government, and the nature and meaning of their work. Challenging assumptions of a universal definition or concept of journalism, the book maps a world populated by a rich diversity of journalistic cultures. Organized around a series of key questions on topics such as editorial autonomy, journalistic ethics, trust in social institutions, and changes in the profession, it details how the practice of journalism differs across the world in a range of political, social, and economic contexts. The book covers how journalism as an institution is created and re-created by journalists and how they experience their profession in very different ways, even as they retain a commitment to some basic, widely shared professional norms and practices. It concludes with a global classification of journalistic cultures that reflects the breadth of worldviews and orientations found in disparate countries and regions. Worlds of Journalism offers an ambitious, comparative global understanding of the state of journalism in a time when it is confronting a series of economic and political threats.

Name: So You Want To Be A Journalist?
Description:
So you want to be a Journalist? Unplugged is a fully revised guide to the world of journalism. This new edition of Bruce Grundy's guide for journalists takes us through new media's impact on the structure and practice of journalism today, with its 24-hour news cycle of multi-platform, interactive media audiences. The book contains instructions on writing for news media as well as practical advice on all facets of reporting. Skills involved in finding information, interviewing, writing news and features, research and investigation, basic subbing, layout and design are covered, along with the essentials of grammar, the law, and practical tips on ethical and professional behaviour. New to the second edition: • online journalism incorporated throughout the text • vignettes and case studies that bring the text to life • examples from Australian, New Zealand and international media • extended section on ethics • extensively updated research section, to help students recognise quality internet research • extensive companion website including further writing practice

Name: News for the Rich, White, and Blue
Description:
In News for the Rich, White, and Blue, Nikki Usher recasts the challenges facing journalism in terms of place, power, and inequality. Drawing on more than a decade of field research, she illuminates how journalists decide what becomes news and how news organizations strategize about the future.

Name: Howard The Duck Masterworks Vol. 1
Description:
Collects Howard the Duck (1976) #1-14; Marvel Treasury Edition (1974) #12; material from Fear (1970) #19; Man-Thing (1974) #1; Giant-Size Man-Thing (1974) #4-5; FOOM (1973) #15. Trapped in a Masterworks he never made! There were several worthy candidates for the milestone 300th Marvel Masterworks, but only one waddled away with victory: Howard the Duck! Steve Gerber and artistic cohorts Val Mayerik, Frank Brunner and Gene Colan crafted one of comics’ most iconoclastic and hilarious characters. Now Howard’s inaugural Masterworks kicks things off with his quirky first appearance as a “fowl out of water,” teaming with the macabre Man-Thing to protect Cleveland from the Man-Frog and Hellcow! The satirical stories continue with Howard and gal-pal Beverly Switzler taking on dire threats like the Space Turnip, the Beaver and — public transportation?! And just wait until Howard runs for president! All restored in Masterworks glory!

Name: Avengers Masterworks Vol. 19
Description:
Collects Avengers (1963) #189-202, Marvel Premiere #55 and Tales of Astonish (1979) #12. Who knew the fight of the Avengers lives would be in the courtroom? Henry Peter Gyrich has his sights set on stripping Earths Mightiest Heroes of their charter  but when the Grey Gargoyle crashes the hearing, the only motion is to Assemble! Then, the action moves to Pittsburgh before a new nemesis arrives in the form of the Taskmaster! In his first appearance, the villain who can replicate any heros actions battles the Avengers, Yellowjacket, the Wasp  and Scott Lang, the all-new Ant-Man! On the way to its milestone 200th issue, the team encounters the towering robot Red Ronin and unites against Ultron in an all-time classic! Also featuring a Wonder Man solo tale, a rare Vision story and an action-packed adventure starring  Jarvis?!

Name: The Jungle’s Treasure
Description:
The Jungle’s Treasure - A Daring Adventure of Unity Get ready for an electrifying journey into the wild with The Jungle’s Treasure—a story that will captivate your imagination and keep you on the edge of your seat! When a colossal serpent guards a long-lost treasure, only the bravery of Benny the wise rabbit, Leo the courageous lion, and their friends can uncover its secrets. But danger lurks in every shadow of the jungle—will they survive the challenges and unlock the treasure’s hidden magic? This 23-page action-packed adventure is bursting with mystery, thrilling twists, and lessons about unity that will leave readers inspired. Why Readers LOVE This Book: Non-Stop Adventure: Each page is filled with excitement and suspense, making it impossible to put down. Timeless Lessons: Teaches kids the value of courage, teamwork, and friendship in a way they’ll never forget. Unforgettable Characters: Benny and Leo will win your heart and ignite your imagination. Instant Download: Once your purchase is complete, you’ll receive immediate access to your ebook in all the formats Perfect For: Young adventurers who crave thrilling stories. Parents looking for an inspiring tale with moral values. Teachers wanting an engaging story for their classrooms. Download Now Dare to uncover the jungle’s secrets! The Jungle’s Treasure is not just a book—it’s an unforgettable experience. Don’t miss out—add it to your cart and start the adventure today!

Name: Spider-Woman Masterworks Vol. 3
Description:
Collects Spider-Woman (1978) #26-38. Publishing magnate Rupert Dockery wants to expand his media empire. To do so will require putting Spider-Woman in her place, but Jessica Drew is not the kind to surrender easily to the machinations of a corporatist like Dockery! Striking an unlikely alliance with the Enforcer may be Spider-Woman's only way to beat back Dockery and save her best friend's life. The saga winds up with the arrival of Werewolf by Night and the stunning debut of the Hornet! Then, in the midst of his Uncanny X-Men glory, Chris Claremont takes over as writer - and he brings the X-Men with him! Jessica must team with Xavier's mutants to take on the unstoppable Juggernaut! Plus: Siryn's first appearance and another classic team-up with Spider-Man!

Name: Marvel Team-Up Masterworks Vol. 7
Description:
Collects Marvel Team-Up (1972) #65-77; material from Marvel Tales (1966) #255, 262-263; Marvel Treasury Edition (1974) #18. It's prime time in the Marvel Universe with Chris Claremont, John Byrne and the amazing Spider-Man! That's right, before they created the definitive X-Men, Claremont and Byrne were spinning tales with the web-slinger. And the historic Spidey exploits they have in store include Captain Britain's introduction to the Marvel U. proper, the first appearance of Arcade, adventures alongside the scintillating Tigra and the malodorous Man-Thing, a two-part tale that revives the Living Monolith and co-stars X-Men Havok and Polaris, and an encounter with Luke Cage and New York's Bravest! Also featured are a mystic turn with Doctor Strange and Clea that features the return of the Silver Dagger; adventures with Cap, Iron Man and Daredevil - and a bevy of bonus material including cover art from MARVEL TALES!

Name: Invincible Iron Man Masterworks Vol. 11
Description:
Collecting Iron Man (1968) #82-94 and Annual #3-4. Tony Stark is one of the Marvel Universes most advanced minds and experienced inventors  but those talents are about to be turned against him, shaking him to his core! Looking to exploit Stark Industries tech, the Red Ghost kidnaps Tony. In the melee, Happy Hogan dons the Iron Man armor, but suffers severe injuries  leading to a dangerous procedure that will once again turn him into the monstrous Freak! Meanwhile, NYPD Detective Michael OBrien begins an investigation aiming to nail Tony for the death of OBriens brother, the Guardsman. Add to that the Blood Brothers, the Controller, the Melter, M.O.D.O.K. and a new incarnation of the Molecule Man  Iron Man must defend himself from threats from all corners before he loses everything!

Name: Spectacular Spider-Man Masterworks Vol. 6
Description:
Collects PETER PARKER, THE SPECTACULAR SPIDER-MAN (1976) #67-79. THE SPECTACULAR SPIDER-MAN hits high gear! Writer Bill Mantlo crafted a compelling, interwoven series of adventures in his second tenure on the title. Pairing Spidey with the Black Cat as both partner and love interest, Mantlo fused the crime drama of the Kingpin with larger-than-life villains like Doctor Octopus - and the formula made SPECTACULAR a page-turning powerhouse! It begins when the Kingpin hires Boomerang, the killer who keeps coming back. Meanwhile, Cloak and Dagger wage war on drugs as the gangster Silvermane clings to life in a new cyborg body! And when Doctor Octopus and the Owl vie to take over New York's criminal underworld, total war breaks out across the city! See why "The Owl/Octopus War" is a saga that ranks among Spider-Man's greatest!

Name: The Red Hot Typewriter
Description:
"This biography of a popular author adds resonance to the body of MacDonald's creative work, as well as providing a deeper understanding of that work that will send reders back to his many books."--BOOK JACKET.

Name: Marvel Two-In-One Masterworks Vol. 4
Description:
Collects Marvel Two-In-One (1974) #37-46 and Annual #2-3, and Avengers Annual #7. Our latest Masterworks offers you two heroes for the price of one! Yes, it's MARVEL TWO-IN-ONE, starring the world's most endearing pile of rocks, the Ever-Lovin' Blue-Eyed Thing! And his adventures begin - behind bars?! The Thing has been declared a public nuisance (and not just on Yancy Street), and it'll take the skills of both Matt Murdock and Daredevil to set him free! This marvelous Masterwork also features the debut of Project PEGASUS, the reality-bending Cosmic Cube, an army of android Visions, the Black Panther and Brother Voodoo vs. a zombie-vampire, Hercules, Captain Marvel - and a bruising Thing vs. Hulk battle! And it's all topped off with Jim Starlin's famous two-part cosmic epic pitting the Avengers and the Thing vs. Thanos and his Infinity Gems!

Name: Digging Up Armageddon
Description:
Preface : "Welcome to Armageddon" - Prologue : "Have Found Solomon's Stables" - Part I. 1920-1926. "Please Accept My Resignation" - "He Must Knock Off or You Will Bury Him" - "A Fairly Sharp Rap on the Knuckles" - "We Have Already Three Distinct Levels" -- Part II. 1927-1934. "I Really Need a Bit of a Holiday" - "They Can Be Nothing Else Than Stables" - "Admonitory but Merciful" - "The Tapping of the Pickmen" - "The Most Sordid Document" - "Either a Battle or an Earthquake" - Part III: 1935-1939. "A Rude Awakening" -- "The Director is Gone" - "You Asked for the Sensational" - "A Miserable Death Threat" - "The Stratigraphical Skeleton" - Part IV: 1940-2020. "Instructions Had Been Given to Protect This Property" - Epilogue "Certain Digging Areas Remain Incompletely Excavated" -- Cast of Characters: Chicago Expedition Staff and Spouses (alphabetical and with participation dates) - Year by Year List of Chicago Expedition Staff plus Major Events.

Name: El DORADO - BOOK 1 - Search for the Lost City
Description:
One of the world's most legendary and elusive treasures, sought after for centuries. . . An ancient mystery.A Lost Treasure.A Hidden City.An impossible location.An unimaginable adventure. Included in Aztec and Mayan legends, Conquistadors had heard rumors of its existence when exploring the New World, but never found it. During World War 2, Nazi inspired archaeologists were convinced they had pinpointed its location. They packed a U-Boat with supplies and set a course for the Amazon Jungle. They disappeared!Many adventurers eager to claim the legendary gold as their own entered one of the most inhospitable places on earth, the Amazon Jungle. Most were never seen again!And yet the exact location of El Dorado and its fantastic hoard of Mayan, Aztec and Inca treasure so many have dreamed of finding, remains a mystery. Any who may have stumbled upon it never returned to tell the tale. It was as if someone, or something, was protecting it... In 1925, Victorian explorer Colonel Percy Fawcett enters the Amazon Jungle to search for a Lost City. Like so many before him, he was never seen or heard from again. Until now!When a message from the past is discovered washed up on an English beach, it reveals new information about the ill-fated 1925 expedition. A modern day expedition sets off to follow in the footsteps of Colonel Fawcett in an attempt to locate the Lost City and its legendary hoard of priceless treasure. El Dorado Book 1 & 2 will take on a journey filled with danger to seek out and enter the fabled Lost City. A thrilling story of adventure and discovery that weaves together an exciting blend of fact and fiction linked to the legends surrounding El Dorado, the lost Fawcett expedition and the mysterious Amazonian Jungle. Rumored to be guarded by remote, mist-veiled mountains, the fabulous treasure hoard was hidden from the greedy clutches of Spanish conquistadors somewhere deep inside the unforgiving and mysterious Amazon jungle. As far as anyone knows, it is still there. Waiting to be discovered by those brave or foolhardy enough, to try their luck. Reviews "This is a terrific two book series set within the atmospherically described Amazon jungle. I could easily imagine myself tagging along with the adventurers." "If you like reading Clive Cussler, Matthew Reilly, James Rollins or Michael Crichton, you will enjoy this action adventure from Ben Hammott." " Has all the ingredients for an instant success: great plot, interesting characters, a large dose of mystery, impressive locations , unexpected twists and discoveries, deception and betrayal and even a touch of romance and a spattering of humor. This story will keep you entertained from beginning to end. Recommended for the permanent library of all action adventure readers." "The Mysterious and Dangerous Amazon Jungle, Subterranean Rooms, Tunnels, Pyramids, Ancient Aliens, Nazis, Traps, Thrilling Escapes, Chases, Strange Creatures, Dangerous Enemies, a Lost City and Great Characters, are just a few ingredients that make this exciting adventure thriller a must read for fans of this genre." (NY.Post.book.reviews) "From bestselling author Ben Hammott this action packed adventure takes you into the Amazon Jungle to follow in the footsteps of lost Victorian Explorer, Colonel Percy Fawcett. What we have here, in part, is an excellent dramatization of what may have happened to Fawcett and what he may have discovered in the unexplored regions of the Amazon. The well written plot is seldom predictable and some of the characters you think are safe, and will be alive by the time the book reaches its climax, are not. Sights and sounds of the Amazon are described well and help to set the atmospheric tone the explorers travel through. A thoroughly enjoyable adventure."An exciting archaeological mystery thriller with flashbacks to Colonel Fawcett's 1925 Expedition.

## The Lost City of the Monkey God: A Honduran Mystery Unveiled

For centuries, the Honduran rainforest has held secrets of a legendary lost city, often called the White City or the Lost City of the Monkey God. Tales passed down from the time of Hernán Cortés speak of a hidden metropolis filled with immense wealth. Indigenous legends describe it as a sacred refuge from Spanish invaders, but also warn of sickness and death for those who venture there.

In 1940, journalist Theodore Morde claimed to have found this city, returning with numerous artifacts. However, his subsequent suicide before revealing its location left the mystery unsolved for decades. Seventy-five years later, author Douglas Preston led a modern expedition utilizing lidar technology. This advanced mapping system pierced the dense rainforest canopy, revealing the unmistakable layout of a vast metropolis in a secluded valley. This discovery provided compelling evidence of an enigmatic, lost civilization.

The expedition into the treacherous rainforest was fraught with peril, including torrential rains, quicksand, venomous creatures, and disease-carrying insects. Upon their return, Preston and other team members contracted a mysterious, sometimes fatal, and incurable illness from the ancient ruins. "The Lost City of the Monkey God" chronicles this remarkable discovery, the perilous journey, and the enduring medical mystery that continues to fascinate.

Name: Atlantis:The Search for the Lost City
Description:
Atlantis:The Search for the Lost City by Mary-Jane Knight, illustrated by Philip Chidlow Readers get caught up in the adventure when a state-of-the-art 19th century submarine sets out on a quest to find the legendary lost city of Atlantis. When danger strikes, a young stowaway comes to their rescue. Readers must follow each clue as the story unfolds, and decode a series of puzzles using the enclosed code wheel. Along the way, they will uncover the history of marine technology & navigation, ancient map-making, and the many fascinating theories behind the mythology of Atlantis.

Name: The Search for Lost Cities
Description:
This major reference is a substantially revised edition of Palmore's International Handbook on Aging, which was voted Choice Outstanding Academic Book of 1980. The reference collects and summarizes information on programs and research in gerontology in most countries where significant work is taking place. The chapters are arranged alphabetically, with each chapter devoted to a particular country. The countries selected represent a wide range of social, political, geographic, and economic conditions, and ten new countries are included in this edition. Each chapter provides current information on the unique features of the country profiled; the roles and status of the aged; problems of the elderly; programs for older adults; research in biomedical, psychological, and sociological aspects of aging; and sources of additional information. Each chapter closes with a list of works for further consultation, and the handbook concludes with an appendix of gerontological associations and a bibliography. Gerontologists, social scientists, and policymakers will find this reference a valuable and current guide to developments and research on aging around the world.

Name: The Lost City of Z
Description:
#1 NEW YORK TIMES BESTSELLER • From the author of Killers of the Flower Moon and The Wager comes a masterpiece of narrative nonfiction “with all the pace and excitement of a movie thriller”(The New York Times) that unravels the greatest exploration mystery of the twentieth century—the story of the legendary British explorer who ventured into the Amazon jungle in search of a fabled civilization and never returned. "[Grann is] one of the preeminent adventure and true-crime writers working today."—New York Magazine After stumbling upon a hidden trove of diaries, acclaimed writer David Grann set out to determine what happened to the British explorer Percy Fawcett and his quest for the Lost City of Z. For centuries Europeans believed the Amazon, the world’s largest rain forest, concealed the glittering kingdom of El Dorado. Thousands had died looking for it, leaving many scientists convinced that the Amazon was truly inimical to humankind. In 1925 Fawcett ventured into the Amazon to find an ancient civilization, hoping to make one of the most important discoveries in history. Then he vanished. Over the years countless perished trying to find evidence of his party and the place he called “The Lost City of Z.” In this masterpiece, journalist David Grann interweaves the spellbinding stories of Fawcett’s quest for “Z” and his own journey into the deadly jungle. Look for David Grann’s latest bestselling book, The Wager!

Name: The Doomed Search for the Lost City of Z
Description:
"Percy Fawcett was a mapmaker and an adventurer. In the early 1900s, he spent years mapping out the jungles of South America. Fawcett became obsessed with the idea of a lost city of gold hidden deep in the jungle. At the age of 57, Fawcett, his 21-year-old son Jack, and Jack's friend Raleigh Rimell left on a quest to find the Lost City of Z. The three men were never heard from again. Untangle the clues they left behind"--

**Unveiling *The Lost City*: A New Saga in Amanda Hocking's Trylle Universe**

Amanda Hocking, celebrated author of the *New York Times* bestselling Kanin Chronicles, returns to the captivating realm of the Trylle Trilogy with her latest novel, *The Lost City*. This eagerly anticipated book ushers in the *Omte Origins* series, offering a profound conclusion to Hocking's beloved saga.

### The Genesis of a Mystery: An Orphan's Beginning

The story commences two decades in the past, amidst a chilling, tempestuous darkness that descended upon a secluded village. A woman, fleeing this encroaching peril, sought solace with local innkeepers. However, by dawn, she had vanished, leaving behind only an infant. Nineteen years later, Ulla Tulin is driven by an unyielding resolve to discover the truth about her parentage and the circumstances of her abandonment.

### The Mimirin and the Pursuit of Heritage

Ulla is convinced that the answers to her identity and her ancestral roots are safeguarded within the hallowed halls of the Mimirin, a prestigious institution dedicated to the scholarly documentation of troll history. Her pursuit of knowledge leads her to secure an internship, where she dedicates herself to translating ancient texts, embarking on a deeply personal quest to unravel her family's lineage. During this endeavor, she finds an unexpected and compelling connection with Pan Soriano, a colleague whose charm and intellect captivate her.

### Encounters and Emerging Threats

Their joint investigation takes a significant and perilous turn with the appearance of Eliana, a young girl who, despite her amnesia, possesses remarkable and seemingly supernatural abilities. Eliana's unique gifts make her a target for relentless bounty hunters, inadvertently drawing Ulla and Pan into a dangerous web of pursuit. As they navigate this treacherous landscape, the lines between folklore and reality blur, with ancient myths manifesting as tangible dangers. This perilous journey may ultimately lead Ulla to the profound revelations she has been searching for.

Name: Lost Civilizations
Description:
Suspenseful, intriguing, simply irresistible: since its debut just a year ago, Mysteries Unwrapped™ has captivated kids. Even reluctant readers love the appealing, high-interest topics and cool "secret file” design that they just have to open. The newest entry to the series introduces children to some of the world’s most fascinating, but now long-gone, civilizations: - Find out about Ur--a thriving ancient city that disappeared under sand - Learn about the Maya and the lengths they went to to appease the gods - Puzzle out the clues to the legendary lost city of Atlantis - Read the graffiti written on the walls of Pompeii before Vesuvius erupted--and preserved intact - See the city "hidden by the Gods”: Angkor Wat, a Khmer temple and religious center in Cambodia

**Exploring Extraterrestrial Possibilities and Unexplained Phenomena**

Jerome Clark's "Hidden Realms, Lost Civilizations, and Beings from Other Worlds" invites readers on a fascinating exploration of topics that have long captured human imagination. The book examines stories of lost lands, mysterious beings from beyond Earth, and the compelling possibility of life beyond our planet. It grounds these explorations in historical accounts and personal stories that challenge common beliefs.

Clark delves into enduring legends, scrutinizing theories about fabled continents like Atlantis and Mu. He also investigates concepts such as subterranean worlds and the idea of advanced civilizations within a Hollow Earth. The book explores the alleged abilities of psychics, like Edward Cayce, and their supposed links to higher consciousness. Additionally, it examines reports of encounters with beings from other solar systems, discussing celestial bodies like Meton, Lanulos, Nibiru, Clarion, Maldek, and Kazik. Clark presents and analyzes accounts of interactions with figures from folklore, such as fairies and goblins, alongside the prophecies of ancient seers. The book also looks into claims of life on Mars and interactions with Martian inhabitants, as well as the mysteries of parallel universes and unexplained aerial phenomena. This comprehensive guide, featuring illustrations and a detailed bibliography, aims to expand your understanding of the extraordinary.

Name: America Before
Description:
The Instant New York Times Bestseller! Was an advanced civilization lost to history in the global cataclysm that ended the last Ice Age? Graham Hancock, the internationally bestselling author, has made it his life's work to find out--and in America Before, he draws on the latest archaeological and DNA evidence to bring his quest to a stunning conclusion. We’ve been taught that North and South America were empty of humans until around 13,000 years ago – amongst the last great landmasses on earth to have been settled by our ancestors. But new discoveries have radically reshaped this long-established picture and we know now that the Americas were first peopled more than 130,000 years ago – many tens of thousands of years before human settlements became established elsewhere. Hancock's research takes us on a series of journeys and encounters with the scientists responsible for the recent extraordinary breakthroughs. In the process, from the Mississippi Valley to the Amazon rainforest, he reveals that ancient "New World" cultures share a legacy of advanced scientific knowledge and sophisticated spiritual beliefs with supposedly unconnected "Old World" cultures. Have archaeologists focused for too long only on the "Old World" in their search for the origins of civilization while failing to consider the revolutionary possibility that those origins might in fact be found in the "New World"? America Before: The Key to Earth's Lost Civilization is the culmination of everything that millions of readers have loved in Hancock's body of work over the past decades, namely a mind-dilating exploration of the mysteries of the past, amazing archaeological discoveries and profound implications for how we lead our lives today.

**Uncover the Secrets of Lost Civilizations with "Hidden History"**

Archaeologist Brian Haughton invites you on a captivating journey through history's most intriguing enigmas in his book, "Hidden History." This expertly researched volume bridges the gap between traditional archaeology and alternative history, offering a common-sense, open-minded exploration of ancient mysteries. Haughton delves into the enduring legacies of lost civilizations, demonstrating how their artifacts and architecture continue to speak to us across millennia.

The book is thoughtfully divided into three compelling sections: Mysterious Places, Unexplained Artifacts, and Enigmatic People. Within these sections, readers will encounter a diverse array of historical puzzles. From the iconic pyramids of Egypt and the remote jungle temples of Peru to the labyrinthine palace of Knossos on Crete and the megalithic wonder of Stonehenge, Haughton brings these enigmatic locations to life.

Beyond well-known sites, "Hidden History" ventures into lesser-known yet equally fascinating historical puzzles. Explore the mystery of the Coso Artifact, ponder the possibility of ancient flight, and unravel the secrets of the enigmatic Voynich Manuscript. The book also introduces readers to intriguing peoples who have captured the imagination, including the ancient Magi, the mystical Druids, the legendary Knights Templar, and the peculiar Green Children.

Featuring over 50 photographs and illustrations, "Hidden History" serves as an ideal reference for anyone fascinated by the archaeology of these great enigmas. Brian Haughton's meticulous research and engaging narrative make this book an essential read for those seeking to understand the profound mysteries that lie beneath the surface of recorded history.

Name: Secrets of the Lost Races
Description:
An increasing number of historical and archaeological finds made around the world have been classified as out-of-place artifacts (ooparts). They have been called this because they appear unexpectedly among the ruins of the past with no evidence of a preceding period of development; their technological sophistication seems far beyond the capabilities of ancient peoples.Drawing on the literature and art of the Chaldeans, Sumerians, Babylonians and others, Rene Noorbergen's contention is that a superior race of man was responsible for these scientific marvels that bear testimony to a civilization with technology comparable to our own.

**Exposed, Uncovered, & Declassified: Lost Civilizations & Secrets of the Past**

This compelling documentary series delves into the enduring mysteries of our ancient world, posing profound questions that challenge conventional historical narratives. Were legendary civilizations like Atlantis and Lemuria more than just myths? What were the true purposes behind the construction of monumental structures like the pyramids? And how sophisticated was the technology wielded by ancient cultures? "Exposed, Uncovered, & Declassified: Lost Civilizations & Secrets of the Past" tackles these enigmatic topics, presenting a collection of original research and explorations that are already igniting passionate discussions among viewers.

The series features a diverse group of experts, each bringing their unique perspectives to unraveling the past. Erich von Däniken, the renowned author of "Chariots of the Gods," meticulously examines the Egyptian pyramids, scrutinizing their astronomical alignments and deciphering the potential messages they were intended to convey. Thomas G. Brophy, PhD, shifts focus to the enigmatic Nabta Playa site in southern Egypt, investigating its profound connections to African history.

Frank Joseph, a seasoned explorer of ancient America, uncovers a series of archaeological scandals and deliberate attempts to suppress crucial evidence. His segment includes the perplexing case of the Smithsonian's alleged "loss" of Maya skulls discovered in the remote Aleutian Islands, highlighting the often-hidden narratives within historical discovery.

Further expanding the scope, researcher Steven Sora, author of "The Lost Colony," presents compelling evidence suggesting that Scotland's ancient Picts may have originated in North America, with potential ties to the indigenous Micmac tribe. Philip Coppens, a familiar face from the History Channel's "Ancient Aliens," explores an ancient Celtic network of roads, proposing a fascinating link to a 4,000-year-old terrestrial replica of Atlantis.

The series also ventures into more esoteric realms. Scholar and mystery enthusiast Oberon Zell-Ravenheart synthesizes ancient lore, connecting the biblical Garden of Eden and the Tree of Life with the cataclysmic event of the great deluge and the submersion of Lemuria. Marie D. Jones and Larry Flaxman, authors of "11:11: The Time Prompt Phenomenon," investigate the ancient civilizations' understanding of sound and resonance, exploring their potential application in constructing megalithic structures and achieving altered states of consciousness. Finally, journalist Nick Redfern sheds light on the U.S. government's persistent interest in our ancient past, delving into religious mysteries and the significance of enigmatic artifacts. The evidence of these ancient enigmas is pervasive, waiting to be discovered by those who know where to look. Whether you approach this subject as a staunch believer, a discerning skeptic, or fall somewhere along the spectrum, "Exposed, Uncovered, and Declassified: Lost Civilizations & Secrets of the Past" promises an enriching and captivating viewing experience.

Name: Lost Cities & Ancient Mysteries of South America
Description:
Rogue adventurer and maverick archaeologist, David Hatcher Childress, takes the reader on unforgettable journeys deep into deadly jungles, windswept mountains and scorching deserts in search of lost civilizations and ancient mysteries.

Name: The Seventy Great Mysteries of the Ancient World
Description:
Describes various issues in mythology and prehistoric and ancient history, from the Garden of Eden to the effects of meteor impacts, including tombs, writing systems, and the fall of civilizations, and suggests explanations.

Name: Europe's Lost Civilization
Description:
Who built megaliths and why? What messages are encoded in the ruins of their spectacular monuments? What light do they throw on our origins and our selves? To answer these questions, Peter Marshal sailed in a small boat from Scotland to Malta and visited all the major megalithic sites on the way. The result is this book. During his epic quest and 4000-mile voyage, Marshall comes up with some starting conclusions and his well-researched book - part adventure story and part historical detective work.

Name: Lost Civilizations
Description:
Unearthing the scientific evidence, myths, and legends of ancient civilization! The reminders of the Ancients are everywhere. They are saved in remnants in archaeology. They are found in reminiscences in mythology. They are recorded in books, story, song, and stone. Who were these people, aliens, man-or-myths? Do we still see their influences today? What remains of these inhabitants of the jungles, lost cities, and dwellings underground, underwater and beyond? How did they rise? Why did they fall? Will they rise again? From pyramids and underground bunkers to watery graves and ancient astronauts, Lost Civilizations: The Secret Histories and Suppressed Technologies of the Ancients examines the archaeological evidence and the traces left behind by more than 70 ancient civilizations, including ... Atlantis Göbekli Tepe Anasazi disappearance in the American Southwest Nazca Lines of Peru Turkey's Çatalhöyük Denisovan Ancestors departure Amazon Cities in the Jungle Neanderthal Ancestors extinction The Eden Stories of Theoretical Physics Underground Cities of the Grand Canyon And many more! From ancient Egypt, middle America, and the Nubian Desert to the frozen Antarctica, underwater ruins of Asia, and clues of visits by ancient aliens, Lost Civilizations explores the unanswered questions about the true origins of man. Might there have been advanced civilizations long before the days of ancient Egypt and Mesopotamia? What do 3D imaging and new underwater mapping technology reveal? What do prehistoric artifacts, architecture, carvings, maps, and monoliths tell us? Were rising waters, erupting volcanoes, catastrophic solar flares, comet or asteroid fragments or some other unimaginable cataclysmic disasters the death of these advanced civilizations? Touring the world and reviewing the scientific evidence, this fascinating book ties together historical events in one part of the world that produced actual effects in others. Uncovering hidden and suppressed pasts of technologically and culturally advanced ancient civilizations, it looks at how modern civilization compares and contrasts to those who have gone before. It will leave you with the sense that what has happened to past advanced civilizations might very well be happening again in our own time! With more than 120 photos and graphics, it is richly illustrated. Its helpful bibliography and extensive index add to its usefulness.

**Uncover the Secrets of Lost Civilizations with "Hidden History"**

"Hidden History" invites you on a captivating journey through the world's most enduring enigmas, blending rigorous archaeological evidence with an open-minded exploration of alternative historical theories. Archaeologist Brian Haughton bridges the gap between established facts and intriguing possibilities, examining how ancient civilizations continue to resonate through their remarkable artifacts and enduring architecture. This expertly researched volume delves into the mysteries of the ancient world, from the sprawling palace of Knossos on Crete and the iconic pyramids of Egypt to the remote jungle temples of Peru and the enigmatic megaliths of Stonehenge.

The book is thoughtfully divided into three compelling sections: Mysterious Places, Unexplained Artifacts, and Enigmatic Peoples. Within these sections, Haughton introduces readers to a diverse array of historical puzzles. Beyond the well-known wonders, he ventures into lesser-known but equally fascinating subjects, such as the perplexing Coso Artifact, the intriguing possibility of ancient flight, and the undeciphered Voynich Manuscript. Furthermore, the book sheds light on mysterious groups and individuals, including the ancient Magi, the enigmatic Druids, the legendary Knights Templar, and the peculiar tale of the Green Children.

Featuring over 50 photographs and illustrations, "Hidden History" serves as an ideal reference for anyone fascinated by the archaeology of these great enigmas. Haughton's common-sense approach, grounded in the latest available data, makes complex historical mysteries accessible and engaging for a broad audience. Whether you're drawn to the grandeur of ancient monuments or the intrigue of unexplained phenomena, this book offers a comprehensive and thought-provoking exploration of history's most compelling secrets.

## America Before: Unearthing a Lost Civilization

**A Groundbreaking Reassessment of Early Human History**

For decades, the prevailing narrative has held that the Americas were settled by humans only around 13,000 years ago, making them among the last continents to be populated. However, a wealth of new archaeological and genetic evidence is dramatically challenging this long-held belief. Internationally bestselling author Graham Hancock, in his seminal work *America Before*, presents a compelling case for a far more ancient and advanced human presence in the Americas, potentially predating established settlements elsewhere by tens of thousands of years.

### Challenging the Timeline: Evidence of Early Peopling

Hancock's extensive research, detailed in *America Before*, synthesizes cutting-edge findings that suggest the Americas were first peopled over 130,000 years ago. This radical revision of the timeline is supported by discoveries that have reshaped our understanding of early human migration and development. The book takes readers on a journey across continents, introducing the scientists and researchers at the forefront of these extraordinary breakthroughs.

### Connecting the Continents: Shared Legacies of Advanced Cultures

Through his explorations, from the fertile Mississippi Valley to the dense Amazon rainforest, Hancock reveals a surprising interconnectedness between ancient "New World" cultures and their "Old World" counterparts. He posits that these seemingly disparate civilizations shared a common legacy of advanced scientific knowledge and sophisticated spiritual beliefs. This raises a critical question: have archaeologists, by focusing predominantly on the "Old World," overlooked the revolutionary possibility that the true origins of civilization lie within the "New World"?

### A Mind-Dilating Exploration of Our Past

*America Before: The Key to Earth's Lost Civilization* represents the culmination of Graham Hancock's decades-long exploration into the mysteries of our planet's past. Drawing on the latest research from institutions like the Smithsonian Institution and insights from leading archaeologists and geneticists, the book offers a mind-dilating exploration of ancient enigmas. It highlights astonishing archaeological discoveries and presents profound implications for how we perceive our present and shape our future. This work is a testament to the enduring power of questioning established narratives and embracing the possibility of a lost chapter in human history.

## Unearthing Ancient Mysteries: Lost Civilizations & Secrets of the Past

This comprehensive exploration delves into the enduring questions surrounding lost civilizations and the secrets of our ancient past. Are legendary places like Atlantis and Lemuria more than just myths? Who were the true architects of the pyramids, and what was their ultimate purpose? How sophisticated was the technology of ancient cultures?

"Exposed, Uncovered, & Declassified: Lost Civilizations & Secrets of the Past" tackles these profound inquiries and more, presenting a collection of original research and compelling theories that are already igniting discussion.

### Exploring Ancient Wonders

*   **Erich von Däniken**, the renowned author of "Chariots of the Gods," examines the Egyptian pyramids, scrutinizing their astronomical alignments and the potential messages they were designed to convey.
*   **Thomas G. Brophy, PhD**, sheds light on the enigmatic Nabta Playa site in southern Egypt, investigating its crucial connections to African history.
*   **Frank Joseph**, a seasoned explorer of ancient America, uncovers archaeological scandals and instances where evidence has been deliberately suppressed, including the perplexing "loss" of Maya skulls from the Aleutian Islands by the Smithsonian.
*   **Steven Sora**, author of "The Lost Colony," presents evidence suggesting that Scotland's Picts may have originated in North America and shared ties with the ancient Micmac tribe.
*   **Philip Coppens**, known for his work on the History Channel's "Ancient Aliens," investigates an ancient Celtic network of roads that could potentially link to a 4,000-year-old terrestrial replica of Atlantis.
*   **Oberon Zell-Ravenheart**, a scholar and explorer of mysteries, connects the biblical Garden of Eden, the Tree of Life, the great deluge, and the submersion of Lemuria.
*   **Marie D. Jones & Larry Flaxman**, authors of "11:11: The Time Prompt Phenomenon," explore ancient civilizations' understanding of sound and resonance, and how these principles might have been employed in constructing megaliths and pyramids, as well as achieving altered states of consciousness.
*   **Journalist Nick Redfern** reveals the U.S. government's persistent interest in our ancient history, religious enigmas, and unexplained artifacts.

The evidence of these ancient mysteries surrounds us, waiting to be discovered by those who know where to look. Whether you approach these topics as a believer, a skeptic, or fall somewhere in between, "Exposed, Uncovered, and Declassified: Lost Civilizations & Secrets of the Past" promises to be both an entertaining and enlightening journey.

**Unearthing Ancient Urban Secrets: A Look at "Four Lost Cities"**

Annalee Newitz's "Four Lost Cities: A Secret History of the Urban Age" offers a compelling journey into the heart of ancient urban life, exploring why some of history's most impressive cities ultimately vanished. This acclaimed work, recognized by NPR and Science Friday, investigates the rise and fall of four distinct ancient metropolises, each a testament to sophisticated civilization.

Newitz guides readers through the Neolithic site of Çatalhöyük in Turkey, the Roman resort town of Pompeii, the medieval Cambodian megacity of Angkor, and the indigenous North American metropolis of Cahokia. By visiting these locations and examining current archaeological findings, she reveals how environmental changes and political turmoil contributed to their abandonment. The book also highlights the crucial, often overlooked, roles of slaves, women, immigrants, and laborers in building these enduring urban centers. "Four Lost Cities" not only revisits the forgotten past but also offers insights into our urbanizing future.

Name: Hidden Cities
Description:
Robert Kennedy, director of the National Park Service, analyzes the discovery of North America and the loss of ancient civilization, from the cities, roads, and commerce of the past as the nation evolved into present day. In Hidden Cities, Robert Kennedy sets out on the bold quest of recovering the rich heritage of the North American peoples through a reimagination of the true relations of their modern-day successors and neighbors. From the Spanish and French explorers that discovered the land that would one day make up the United States to present day in the country, very few Euro-Americans have paid attention to the evidence and meaning of the nation’s heritage. As Kennedy shows the magnificence of the mound-building cultures through the sometimes prejudiced eyes of the founding generation, he reveals the astounding history of the North American continent in a way that sheds important light on the credit Native American predecessors deserve but many refuse to give.

Name: The Lost Civilization Enigma
Description:
An examination of archaeological controversies, with arguments showing evidence of prehistoric civilizations not recorded as part of human history. Are history books giving us the whole story? Or is civilization far more complex and older than we have been taught? Our school textbooks barely mention the 6,000-year-old Sumerian civilization, yet the latest archaeological findings at sites such as Jericho and, most recently, Gobekli Tepe in Turkey have been dated to 10,000 BC. Civilization goes back at least another 10,000 years, if we are willing to believe what our ancestors themselves claimed. The Lost Civilization Enigma reveals the truth about: Lost magnitudes to known cultures, such as the Bosnian Pyramids and the civilization of “Old Europe.” The fabled lost “golden” cities of South America and the Amazon, which are slowly being rediscovered. Fascinating examples of lost technology, such as the Antikythera Device. Atlantis and the fact that it was a real civilization. Analyzing the historical and archaeological record, best-selling author Philip Coppens demonstrates that there is substantial evidence that civilization is far older, far more advanced, and far more special than is currently accepted. Clearly, our history books have left out a great deal!

Name: Disinformation Guide to Ancient Aliens, Lost Civilizations, Astonishing Archaeology & Hidden History
Description:
"If you think the history you were taught in school was accurate, you're in for a big surprise. This group of researchers blows the lid off everything you thought you knew about the origins of the human race and the culture we live in"--Cover p. [4].

Name: The Source Field Investigations
Description:
Prepare yourself for a revealing tour through the most incredible scientific mysteries of the world with your guide David Wilcock, the New York Times bestselling author of Awakening in the Dream. More than two million people have seen David Wilcock’s incredible tour of the 2012 prophecies in his Internet documentary, The 2012 Enigma. Now, he expands his vision with a cutting-edge investigation into alternative sciences with deep insights into what is coming in our immediate future. A stunning synthesis of hidden science and lost prophecies, The Source Field Investigations exposes DNA transformation, wormholes, ancient conspiracies, the Maya calendar, and a new model of galactic energy fields triggering mental, biological, and spiritual evolution. Unlike the apocalyptic viewpoints depicted in big-budget disaster films, Wilcock believes that 2012 will be a watermark for widespread acceptance of a greater reality—and here, he lays out the blueprints for such a Golden Age.

Name: Secrets of the Lost Races
Description:
An increasing number of historical and archaeological finds made around the world have been classified as out-of-place artifacts (ooparts). They have been called this because they appear unexpectedly among the ruins of the past with no evidence of a preceding period of development; their technological sophistication seems far beyond the capabilities of ancient peoples.Drawing on the literature and art of the Chaldeans, Sumerians, Babylonians and others, Rene Noorbergen's contention is that a superior race of man was responsible for these scientific marvels that bear testimony to a civilization with technology comparable to our own.

Name: Wings of Fire Collection
Description:
A thrilling new series soars above the competition and redefines middle-grade fantasy fiction for a new generation! The seven dragon tribes have been at war for generations, locked in an endless battle over an ancient, lost treasure. A secret movement called the Talons of Peace is determined to bring an end to the fighting, with the help of a prophecy--a foretelling that calls for great sacrifice. Five dragonets are collected to fulfill the prophecy, raised in a hidden cave and enlisted, against their will, to end the terrible war. But not every dragonet wants a destiny. And when the select five escape their underground captors to look for their original homes, what has been unleashed on the dragon world may be far more than the revolutionary planners intended... This must-have collection includes the first three novels in the Wings of Fire series: * Wings of Fire Book One: The Dragonet Prophecy *Wings of Fire Book Two: The Lost Heir *Wings of Fire Book Three: The Hidden Kingdom

## Lost Kingdom: A Chronicle of Imperial Ambition and Hawaiian Sovereignty

**A Gripping Account of Big Sugar's Influence**

The New York Times–bestselling author presents "Lost Kingdom," a riveting saga that vividly portrays the imperialist ambitions of "Big Sugar" in Hawaii. This exceptional book, lauded by Douglas Brinkley as "a real gem," masterfully intertwines the stories of a diverse cast of characters. It brings to life the profound conflict between the vulnerable Polynesian people and the relentless expansion of capitalist powers.

**The Rise and Fall of a Monarchy**

Through compelling portraits of royalty, rogues, influential sugar barons, and dedicated missionaries, "Lost Kingdom" unfolds as a sweeping narrative of the Hawaiian Kingdom's ascent and eventual decline. At the heart of this historical drama is Lili‘uokalani, the last queen of Hawai‘i. Born in 1838, her life spanned a period of dramatic economic transformation for the islands.

**Economic Transformation and Political Manipulation**

Lucrative sugar plantations steadily consumed the majority of Hawaiian land, with ownership almost exclusively held by white planters who became known as the "Sugar Kings." This burgeoning economic power transformed Hawai‘i into a coveted prize in the geopolitical contest between America, Britain, and France, each vying for greater military and commercial influence in the Pacific. The Hawaiian monarchy, once a symbol of sovereignty, was increasingly reduced to a figurehead, vulnerable to the machinations of wealthy sugar plantation owners.

**Queen Lili‘uokalani's Struggle for Power**

Queen Lili‘uokalani was resolutely determined to enact a new constitution that would restore the monarchy's diminished power. However, her efforts were ultimately thwarted by the United States. This marked the beginning of Hawai‘i's annexation, ushering in a new era of American imperialism.

**A Little-Known Chapter of National History**

As The New York Times Book Review notes, "Lost Kingdom" illuminates "an important chapter in our national history, one that most Americans don’t know but should." Fortune magazine praises Siler's work for offering "a riveting and intimate look at the rise and tragic fall of Hawaii’s royal family," serving as a poignant reminder of Hawaii's enduring beauty, even as its kingdom was lost. The Los Angeles Times further commends the book as a "well-researched, nicely contextualized history," describing the events as indeed "'one of the most audacious land grabs of the Gilded Age.'"

Name: The Lost Kingdoms of the Nakshatras
Description:
I am practicing astrology from my childhood and with multiple decades of experience I am presenting in this book a story of 27 nakshatras in story telling format to understand it more deeply. Astrology is a divine science and everybody should learn it to understand the divine code of this universe. How our karma of past life manifest in to the present life and it reflects in our horoscope. Horoscope is nothing but snapshot of our karma of past life. I hope people will love reading this and I welcome everyone to dive in to this divine knowledge. Regards, Saket Shah

Name: Cavelli's Lost Heir
Description:
Normally Prince Nico Cavelli would never waste his time visiting the prison cell of a tourist. Except this particular alleged criminal has stolen something very personal to him-- his son, heir to the Montebianco throne Lily Morgan always knew it was a mistake coming to the Mediterranean kingdom, but she'd had no choice. First she was thrown into jail for a crime she didn't commit...now she's been bailed out by the prince--though in return she must become his royal wife

Name: Swimming in the Deep End
Description:
"In this moving, masterful saga from best-selling novelist Christina Suzann Nelson, four women's lives collide. Their tangled courses and attempts to find grace in the midst of crisis combine in one heartfelt story showing the many faces of motherly love"--

Name: The Lost Heir
Description:
NY Times Bestselling Author E.G. Foley brews up a magical potion for fantasy readers of all ages! Take a dash of Harry Potter and a splash of Oliver Twist, add a pinch of steampunk and a sprinkle of Victorian fairy lore, and what do you get? The Gryphon Chronicles! A rollicking fantasy adventure series that's as much fun for grownups as it is for kids. Strange new talents... Jake is a scrappy orphaned pickpocket living by his wits on the streets of Victorian London. Lately he's started seeing ghosts and discovers he can move solid objects with his mind! He has no idea why. Next thing he knows, a Sinister Gentleman and his minions come hunting him. On the run for his life, Jake is plunged headlong into a mysterious world full of magic and deadly peril. A world that holds the secret to who he really is: the long-lost heir of an aristocratic family - with magical powers!
