But with treacherous enemies closing in, it will take all of his wily street instincts and the help of his friends - both human and magical - to solve the mystery of what happened to his parents, and defeat the foes who never wanted the Lost Heir of Griffon to be found... (270 pages / 100,409 words)

The second installment of the WINGS OF FIRE saga, titled "The Lost Heir," plunges readers into a captivating underwater realm, unraveling a mystery that promises to reshape the destiny of the SeaWing kingdom. This thrilling adventure centers on Tsunami, the long-lost heir to the SeaWing throne, as she finally embarks on her journey home. The anticipation is palpable as Tsunami, alongside her fellow dragonets of destiny, ventures beneath the waves to reach the magnificent SeaWing Kingdom.

Having been stolen as an egg from the royal hatchery, Tsunami is filled with eagerness to meet her future subjects and, most importantly, to be reunited with her mother, Queen Coral. However, Tsunami's much-anticipated homecoming is far from the joyous occasion she envisioned. While Queen Coral extends a warm welcome, a sinister shadow looms over the kingdom. A relentless and mysterious assassin has been systematically eliminating the queen's heirs for years, and Tsunami finds herself in grave danger, potentially becoming the next victim. The dragonets sought refuge and protection among the SeaWings, but this seemingly serene ocean is a treacherous expanse, concealing a labyrinth of secrets, insidious betrayal, and the ever-present threat of death.

Name: Lost Heirs
Description:
Prophecy and destiny combine to tear peasant-born twins, Ilan and Adra from their home and return them to the throne their grandfather refused. Compelled to stay by the needs of their people; driven to go by their resentful nobles and hostile neighbors, Ilan can't forget his duty while Adra only wants her freedom back. Regardless of how they choose, they stand to lose but if they can't agree, they are going to lose each other and the country has its own plans for them.

## Ancient Faiths Enduring in the Modern Middle East

The Middle East, often seen as religiously uniform, is actually a vibrant tapestry of ancient and unique faiths. These traditions, with beliefs ranging from divine Greek prophets to Lucifer as a peacock deity and ancient reincarnation cycles, are living links to civilizations like Persia, Babylon, and Pharaonic Egypt. For centuries, their followers have safeguarded their beliefs through invasions and assimilation pressures. Today, however, regional instability poses significant threats to their continued existence.

Former diplomat Gerard Russell's book, "Heirs to Forgotten Kingdoms," explores these hidden worlds. He journeys to remote communities, including the Mandaeans and Ezidis in Iraq, Zoroastrians in Iran, and Copts in Egypt. Russell shares their rich histories, participates in their rituals, and highlights the dangers they confront. The rise of extremist groups and Western interventions, alongside younger generations seeking opportunities abroad, create an existential challenge for these minority religions. Russell's research provides a crucial look at their past, present, and uncertain future.

Name: The Highly Sensitive Person in Love
Description:
Do you fall in love hard, but fear intimacy? Are you sick of being told that you are “too sensitive”? Do you struggle to respect a less-sensitive partner? Or have you given up on love, afraid of being too sensitive or shy to endure its wounds? Statistics show that 50 percent of what determines divorce is genetic temperament. And, if you are one of the 20 percent of people who are born highly sensitive, the risk of an unhappy relationship is especially high. Your finely tuned nervous system, which picks up on subtleties and reflects deeply, would be a romantic asset if both you and your partner understood you better. But without that understanding, your sensitivity is likely to be making your close relationships painful and complicated. Based on Elaine N. Aron’s groundbreaking research on temperament and intimacy, The Highly Sensitive Person in Love offers practical help for highly sensitive people seeking happier, healthier romantic relationships. From low-stress fighting to sensitive sexuality, the book offers a wealth of practical advice on making the most of all personality combinations. Complete with illuminating self-tests and the results of the first survey ever done on sex and temperament, The Highly Sensitive Person in Love will help you discover a better way of living and loving.

Name: Love, Sex, and Lasting Relationships
Description:
God wants his children to have a lasting relationship and great sex--the results of a deep, meaningful love that is rooted in commitment. Now updated and with a fresh new cover, Love, Sex, and Lasting Relationships helps readers walk a path to true love that is more fulfilling than they ever imagined. "There's a better way to find love, stay in love, and grow in intimacy for a lifetime," says Chip Ingram. It's God's way. Whether single or married, happy or searching for hope, readers will discover that by following God's prescription, they can create a love that lasts.

## Finding Perfect Love in Imperfect Relationships

Many of us experience moments of deep, open love, but sustaining it in our intimate relationships can be a challenge. If love is so powerful, why do our relationships often feel difficult? If it brings happiness, why do we struggle to fully embrace it?

In "Perfect Love, Imperfect Relationships," renowned couples therapist John Welwood explores these questions. He identifies a single, universal cause for relationship problems: a "wound of the heart." This wound creates a pervasive feeling of unlove, a deep-seated belief that we are not inherently lovable as we are. This lack of self-acceptance hinders our ability to trust and fully open ourselves to love, even when we deeply desire it.

### The Root of Relational Challenges

This core wound impacts not only our personal connections but also the overall quality of life in our world. It manifests as a persistent mood of unlove, making it difficult to let love flow freely.

### A Path to Healing and Transformation

Welwood's book guides readers on a transformative journey. It teaches us to embrace imperfections, both within ourselves and our relationships, as stepping stones toward greater love. The book offers a process for releasing grievances we hold against others for not loving us adequately, and against ourselves for not being loved better.

### Embracing Love's True Nature

Ultimately, "Perfect Love, Imperfect Relationships" reveals how our longing for love can attract the very love that frees us. By understanding ourselves and our relational patterns, we can find profound healing and contribute to a more loving world. This revolutionary book provides practical guidance for navigating the complexities of love with realism and lyrical insight.

Name: Get the Guy
Description:
Most dating books tell you what NOT to do. Here's a book dedicated to telling you what you CAN do. In his book, Get the Guy, Matthew Hussey—relationship expert, matchmaker, and star of the reality show Ready for Love—reveals the secrets of the male mind and the fundamentals of dating and mating for a proven, revolutionary approach to help women to find lasting love. Matthew Hussey has coached thousands of high-powered CEOs, showing them how to develop confidence and build relationships that translate into professional success. Many of Matthew’s male clients pressed him for advice on how to apply his winning strategies not to just get the job, but how to get the girl. As his reputation grew, Hussey was approached by more and more women, eager to hear what he had learned about the male perspective on love and romance. From landing a first date to establishing emotional intimacy, playful flirtation to red-hot bedroom tips, Matthew’s insightfulness, irreverence, and warmth makes Get the Guy: Learn Secrets of the Male Mind to Find the Man You Want and the Love You Deserve a one-of-a-kind relationship guide and the handbook for every woman who wants to get the guy she’s been waiting for.

Name: Anatomy of Love
Description:
An exploration of human behavior examines the innate aspects of love, sex, and marriage, discussing flirting behavior, courting postures, the brain chemistry of attraction, divorce and adultery in societies around the world, and more. Reprint.

Name: Mixed-Up Love
Description:
Dating, commitment, kids, and family--it's all hard work, and when you come from different religious backgrounds it's even harder. Jon, a Catholic writer, and Michal, a Reconstructionist rabbi, live out the challenges of an interfaith relationship everyday as husband and wife, and as parents to their daughter Sima, who is being raised Jewish. In MIXED-UP LOVE, the couple explores how interfaith relationships impact dating, weddings, holidays, raising children, and family functions--and how to not just cope, but thrive. This is an engaging and practical resource for singles who are considering dating outside their own faith, couples in interfaith relationships, relatives and friends of "mixed" couples who seek information and understanding, and parents desiring a fresh perspective. With clarity, insight, and humor, Sweeney and Woll demonstrate how to engage with your partner, family, and faith like never before.

## Finding Lasting Love: Susan Piver's Wisdom for Modern Romance

Are you struggling with the challenges of modern relationships? Do you wonder why love can be so difficult, leading to heartbreak, resentment, or even divorce? Susan Piver, a New York Times bestselling author and respected mindfulness expert, presents a powerful and life-changing way to understand and improve your romantic connections. She draws on ancient Buddhist teachings and applies them to today's relationships, offering fresh perspectives that can transform how you connect with yourself and others.

### Rethinking Romance and Relationships

Piver explores the reasons why love often feels out of reach. She examines common problems in dating, sex, and romance. By looking at the habits, past hurts, and expectations that often get in the way of our connections, she helps readers find a more open and genuine way to experience love. This mindful approach encourages you to open your heart without fear. It leads to better communication with your partner and builds more compassion and strength. Ultimately, Piver's ideas show a way to true happiness. They offer the possibility of deep, fulfilling love for yourself and for others. As Seth Godin, author of *Linchpin*, says about Piver, she is "a generous, caring, loving teacher, someone with an open heart and a clear mind, eager to help us find our own way forward."

**Essays in Love: A Philosophical Exploration of Romantic Relationships**

Alain de Botton's *Essays in Love* offers a profound and unique perspective on the complexities of romantic relationships, blending the narrative of a captivating love story with insightful philosophical inquiry. Rooted in the mysterious workings of the human heart and mind, this classic work delves into the very essence of what it means to connect with another person. As Sheila Heti notes in her introduction, the book illuminates emotions that are frequently experienced but seldom articulated.

**The Genesis of Connection**

The narrative unfolds with a chance encounter between a man and a woman on a flight from Paris to London. What begins as casual conversation blossoms into a full-fledged love story, charting the course from the initial spark of attraction to the inevitable disagreements, the soaring highs of elation, and the crushing lows of heartbreak. De Botton masterfully dissects each phase of their relationship, revealing the intricate emotional landscape that defines human connection.

**Unraveling the Mysteries of the Heart**

With the narrative flair of a novelist and the sharp intellect of a philosopher, de Botton dissects the often-unspoken dynamics of love. He explores the idea that our sense of self might be intrinsically linked to being perceived and understood by another. The book posits that perhaps we are not fully alive until we are loved, suggesting that love is a crucial element in our self-realization. *Essays in Love* is celebrated for its ability to illuminate these often-felt but rarely understood emotions, making it an essential read for anyone who has experienced the transformative power of falling in love.

Name: Love Sense
Description:
The bestselling author of Hold Me Tight presents a revolutionary new understanding of why and how we love, based on cutting-edge research. Every day, we hear of relationships failing and questions of whether humans are meant to be monogamous. Love Sense presents new scientific evidence that tells us that humans are meant to mate for life. Dr. Johnson explains that romantic love is an attachment bond, just like that between mother and child, and shows us how to develop our "love sense" -- our ability to develop long-lasting relationships. Love is not the least bit illogical or random, but actually an ordered and wise recipe for survival. Love Sense covers the three stages of a relationship and how to best weather them; the intelligence of emotions and the logic of love; the physical and psychological benefits of secure love; and much more. Based on groundbreaking research, Love Sense will change the way we think about love.

Name: Getting the Love You Want
Description:
I know of no better guide for couples who genuinely desire a maturing relationship.M. Scott Peck, author of The Road Less Traveled A remarkable bookthe most incisive and persuasive I have ever read on the knotty problems of marriage relationships. Ann Roberts, former president, Rockefeller Family Fund

The Kanwar pilgrimage stands as India's most significant annual religious journey. Each year, millions of devoted individuals embark on a sacred quest, collecting holy water from the Ganga River. They then transport this water over hundreds of miles, a arduous trek culminating in its offering at Śiva shrines. These pilgrims, often referred to as "bhola" (meaning gullible or fools) and sometimes viewed as troublemakers by mainstream society, are predominantly young, impoverished men. They represent a segment of the population often marginalized by the forces of globalization and economic shifts.

However, for these young men, the demanding Kanwar pilgrimage is far from a senseless endeavor. Instead, it serves as a profound mechanism for confronting and mastering their anxieties. It is a powerful way to demonstrate their sincerity and commitment in the face of challenging social circumstances.

Vikash Singh's immersive experience walking alongside the Kanwar procession is documented in his book. Through this work, he illuminates how the pilgrimage functions as a vital social arena. Within this space, participants have the opportunity to showcase their inherent talents, unwavering resolve, and intrinsic moral value.

Singh's analysis draws from a rich interdisciplinary foundation, incorporating social theory, phenomenology, Indian metaphysics, and psychoanalysis. He argues that the pilgrimage offers a unique environment where participants can simultaneously reconstruct their identities and prepare themselves for the realities of a precarious, informal economy and the inherent uncertainties of social life.

By identifying with Śiva, a deity who embodies both supreme mastery of the world and a seemingly disheveled, even pitiable, persona, the pilgrims assert their own sense of sovereignty and desirability. This act of identification allows them to transcend their stigmatized social positions. "Uprising of the Fools" ultimately reveals that contemporary religious practice is not merely a retreat into established traditions. Rather, it functions as a dynamic forum for seeking recognition and enacting resistance within the pervasive landscape of global neoliberalism.

Name: Automata and Mimesis on the Stage of Theatre History
Description:
The automaton, known today as the robot, can be seen as a metaphor for the historical period in which it is explored. Chapters include examinations of Iconoclasm's fear that art might surpass nature, the Cartesian mind/body divide, automata as objects of courtly desire, the uncanny Olympia, and the revolutionary Robots in post-WWI drama.

## Unlocking Artificial General Intelligence: Lessons from Natural Minds

The quest for Artificial General Intelligence (AGI)—systems capable of human-like thought, learning, and action—is a central ambition in computer science. Fueled by recent breakthroughs in machine learning, this pursuit prompts critical inquiries: What defines a truly general intelligence? What algorithmic foundations, architectural designs, and cognitive mechanisms are indispensable for its creation? To navigate these complex questions, a profound examination of **Natural General Intelligence** becomes paramount.

By studying the cognitive capabilities inherent in humans and other species, we gain invaluable insights into the hallmarks of intelligence that AI researchers strive to replicate. Disciplines like cognitive science and neuroscience offer a framework for dissecting these natural intelligence components, conceptualizing them as intricate computational processes and cognitive functions orchestrated within neural circuits. The book "Natural General Intelligence" delves into this comparison, scrutinizing the algorithms and architectures that propel AI advancements by contrasting them with the intricate workings of biological brains. This comparative analysis evaluates the current standing of AI systems against their natural counterparts, probing fundamental conceptual challenges within perceptual, memory, and control systems, while also exploring the very essence of human cognition and knowledge organization.

This interdisciplinary endeavor not only addresses the ongoing discourse surrounding the definition of intelligence but also critically examines the extent to which AI research should draw inspiration from biological systems. The ultimate aim is to bridge the theoretical understanding of neuroscientists and cognitive scientists, who meticulously study biological brains, with the practical innovations of AI researchers constructing artificial ones. This collaborative approach, bolstered by the contributions of luminaries such as **Professor Michael I. Jordan** and the groundbreaking research from the **Allen Institute for Brain Science**, promises a more profound comprehension of the pathway toward achieving AGI.

## Ancient China's Mechanical Engineering Innovations

Ancient China made significant contributions to mechanical engineering, harnessing physical principles for power and control. Joseph Needham's "Science and Civilisation in China," Volume 4, Part 2, details these advancements, moving beyond simple tools to explore sophisticated applications of physics.

A key influence was the concept of "chhi," similar to the Greek "pneuma." This pneumatic mindset fueled remarkable mechanical inventions. Notable examples include the double-acting piston-bellows for consistent airflow, the rotary winnowing-fan for efficient grain processing, and the water-powered metallurgical blowing-machine. This last invention, a precursor to the steam engine, used water power to drive bellows for high-temperature furnaces, greatly improving metalworking. The Chinese also showed early insights into aeronautics, demonstrating a deep understanding of physical forces and their potential.

Name: The Fourth Discontinuity
Description:
Discusses the relationship between humans and machines, pondering the implications of humans becoming more mechanical and of computer robots being programmed to think. He describes early Greek and Chinese automatons and discusses ideas of previous centuries and of individuals on this subject.

## Unlock the Future with Robotics

Robotics is a rapidly advancing field that's changing industries and our daily lives. This guide, "Mastering Robotics," is your key to understanding and using this exciting technology, which combines science, engineering, and creativity.

### The Building Blocks of Robotics

This resource explores the core concepts of robotics, a vital part of modern automation. Whether you're a beginner or have some experience, you'll gain the knowledge to design, build, and use robotic systems effectively.

#### Essential Components and Function

Learn the basic principles of how robots work, including their key parts and how they interact with their surroundings.

#### How Robots Move

Understand the mechanics behind robotic movement, such as kinematic chains, which explain how robots achieve specific positions and motions.

#### Seeing and Understanding the World

Discover how robots use sensors to perceive and interpret their environment, allowing them to make sense of what's around them.

#### Controlling Robot Actions

Explore the programming side of robotics, learning how to write code that directs a robot's movements and reactions.

#### Advanced Robot Intelligence

Dive into areas like robot vision and machine learning, where robots learn to process visual information and adapt their behavior using smart algorithms.

#### Navigating and Mapping Environments

Grasp the importance of localization and mapping, which allow robots to know where they are and create maps of their surroundings.

#### Performing Tasks and Handling Objects

Learn how robots manipulate objects and execute tasks, focusing on control and stability.

### Robotics in Action and What's Next

See how robotics is used in many areas, from factories to hospitals. Understanding robotics gives you an edge in today's fast-paced tech world. This guide helps engineers, researchers, and tech fans use robotics to drive innovation and create solutions that improve human abilities and transform industries.

Start your journey to master robotics and gain the skills to design, build, and deploy systems that push the limits of what's possible.

© 2023 Cybellium Ltd. All rights reserved. www.cybellium.com

## Navigating Emotions in Late Modernity

Contemporary society, characterized by its emphasis on individualism and self-awareness, has profoundly reshaped how we experience and express our emotions. The international collection, *Emotions in Late Modernity*, offers a comprehensive exploration of these shifts, delving into the complex emotional terrain of our current era.

This collection challenges conventional boundaries in understanding emotions. It questions the rigid separation of feeling from logic, the simplistic categorization of emotions as merely "good" or "bad," and the artificial disciplinary divides between sociology, philosophy, and psychology when examining emotion, ethics, and gender. Furthermore, it re-evaluates traditional notions of love and solitude within the context of modern life.

*Emotions in Late Modernity* investigates the intricate relationship between collective and individual emotional experiences. This is exemplified by the concept of "emotional sharing" and the increasing legal accountability for anger-related offenses. The book also examines how enthusiasm and emotions associated with achievement are cultivated in educational environments.

A significant focus is placed on the pervasive impact of digital technology on our emotional lives. The collection contrasts older forms of emotional connection, such as letters and face-to-face interactions, with contemporary digital mediation. This mediation is analyzed across various contexts, from classroom instruction and large public gatherings like protests to the portrayal of emotions in film and documentaries.

The collection also addresses the deliberate management of emotions, both on a personal and societal level. This includes an examination of the "politics of fear" surrounding refugees and religious groups, as well as collective responses to climate change.

As a seminal work on the evolving nature of emotional experience, *Emotions in Late Modernity* is an essential resource for students and researchers in fields like the sociology of emotions, cultural studies, political science, and psychology. Chapter 2 is available as a free Open Access PDF download under a CC-BY-NC-ND 4.0 license at http://www.taylorfrancis.com.

Name: The Romantic Machine
Description:
Introduction: Mechanical Romanticism -- DEVICES OF COSMIC UNITY -- Ampère's Experiments: Contours of a Cosmic Cubstance -- Humboldt's Instruments: Even the Tools Will Be Free -- Arago's Daguerreotype: The Labor Theory of Knowledge -- SPECTACLES OF CREATION AND METAMORPHOSIS -- The Devil's Opera: Fantastic Physiospiritualism -- Monsters, Machine-Men, Magicians: The Automaton in the Garden -- ENGINEERS OF ARTIFICIAL PARADISES -- Saint-Simonian Engines: Love and Conversions -- Leroux's Pianotype: The Organogenesis of Humanity -- Comte's Calendar: From Infinite Universe to Closed World -- Conclusion: Afterlives of the Romantic Machine.

Name: Mindfulness and Meditation at University
Description:
Why should mindfulness and meditation be taught at universities? What impact could the establishment of such programs have on students and on the education system itself? Andreas de Bruin showcases the remarkable results of the first ten years of the Munich Model »Mindfulness and Meditation in a University Context« - a program started in the year 2010 in which 2000 students have already participated. Through meditation-journal entries featured in the book, students describe the effects of mindfulness and meditation on their studies and in their daily lives. In addition to an overview of cutting-edge research into mindfulness and meditation, along with in-depth analyses and explanations of key terms, the book also contains numerous practical exercises with instructions.

**Practical Mindfulness: Navigating Stress in Uncertain Times**

In today's world, feeling overwhelmed by stress is a common experience. This accessible meditation guide offers a practical pathway to adapt and find your inner peace. It provides an insightful and demystifying look at mindfulness practice, earning recognition as a 2021 INDIES Gold Winner in the Body, Mind & Spirit category.

**Discover Life-Changing Benefits and Practical Techniques**

"Practical Mindfulness" approaches meditation with a hands-on, how-to perspective, making it ideal for anyone curious about the practice. Whether you're seeking to manage daily stressors or are a healthcare or education professional looking to teach foundational meditation skills, this book delivers. Dr. Sazima's training routines offer effective coping methods to combat burnout and foster resilience amidst life's challenges.

**Reclaim Your Inner Sanctuary**

We all yearn for a sense of peace and security, an internal "home." When faced with adversity, from work-related pressures to significant health issues, the limitations of external solutions become apparent. Without cultivating a deeper internal awareness, stress, anxiety, and depression can take hold. This guide serves as your solution, empowering you to reclaim your peaceful center in every moment.

**Expert Guidance from a Personal Journey**

Dr. Sazima, a board-certified psychiatrist and educator at Stanford's Family Medicine Residency, brings a wealth of experience as both a meditator and teacher. His personal journey, including navigating a rare bone cancer diagnosis, highlights the profound power of meditation. Now, he is dedicated to sharing this transformative practice, explaining its efficacy in an accessible and engaging manner. "Practical Mindfulness" demonstrates that adaptation is possible. Readers who have enjoyed titles like "Think Like A Monk," "Breath: The New Science of a Lost Art," or "10% Happier" will find immense value in this guide.

Name: Practicing Mindfulness
Description:
Find peace and calm in the new year with simple mindfulness meditations Mindfulness is an evidence-based method for reducing stress and anxiety, enhancing resilience, and maintaining mental well-being. Even short meditations can turn a bad day around, ground us in the present moment, and help us approach life with gratitude and kindness. Practicing Mindfulness was created by the founder of One Mind Dharma. He developed these exercises to offer practical guidance for anyone who wants to realize the benefits of mindful meditation - this year and beyond. EVIDENCE-BASED ADVICE: Find expert advice on dealing with distorted or wandering thoughts and how to handle mental blocks. MEDITATIONS THAT GROW WITH YOU: The meditations in Practicing Mindfulness can take as few as 5 minutes, and the lessons build on each other, helping you develop a transformative mindfulness practice. MEDITATIONS FOR SPECIFIC SITUATIONS: With meditations designed for specific situations or emotions, even experienced practitioners who have trained their mind will have an essential resource for mindfulness at every moment. Begin a journey of peace and patience this year with Practicing Mindfulness.

Name: Mindfulness
Description:
The life-changing international bestseller reveals a set of simple yet powerful mindfulness practices that you can incorporate into daily life to help break the cycle of anxiety, stress, unhappiness, and exhaustion. Mindfulness promotes the kind of happiness and peace that gets into your bones. It seeps into everything you do and helps you meet the worst that life throws at you with new courage. Based on Mindfulness-Based Cognitive Therapy (MBCT), the book revolves around a straightforward form of mindfulness meditation which takes just a few minutes a day for the full benefits to be revealed. MBCT has been clinically proven to be at least as effective as drugs for depression and is widely recommended by US physicians and the UK's National Institute for Health and Clinical Excellence—in other words, it works. More importantly it also works for people who are not depressed but who are struggling to keep up with the constant demands of the modern world. MBCT was developed by the book's author, Oxford professor Mark Williams, and his colleagues at the Universities of Cambridge and Toronto. By investing just 10 to 20 minutes each day, you can learn the simple mindfulness meditations at the heart of MBCT and fully reap their benefits. The book includes links to audio meditations to help guide you through the process. You'll be surprised by how quickly these techniques will have you enjoying life again.

Name: Mindfulness and Meditation
Description:
From hormones to homework, parents to peers, health issues to bad habits, life can be a pressure cooker. How can we find relief? Author Whitney Stewart introduces readers to the practice of mindfulness. With its roots in ancient Buddhist teachings, mindfulness--the practice of purposefully focusing attention on the present moment--can change a person's approach to stress, develop skills to handle anxiety and depression, and provide a sense of awareness and belonging. Stewart guides readers through how to get started with meditation as well as provides specific exercises for examining emotions, managing stress, checking social media habits and wellness routines, and setting intentions to increase happiness. "A thorough and accessible resource for young people."--Kirkus Reviews "Considering the range of challenges that today's teens face, equipping them with pertinent coping skills is crucial for their future success and well-being. . . . [S]traightforward and refreshing."--School Library Journal

Name: The Miracle of Mindfulness
Description:
A new gift edition of the classic guide to meditation and mindfulness, featuring archival photography and beautiful calligraphy by Thich Nhat Hanh The Miracle of Mindfulness is a classic guide to meditation that has introduced generations of readers to the life-changing beauty of mindful awareness. Zen Master Thich Nhat Hanh offers gentle anecdotes and practical exercise as a means of learning the skills of mindfulness. From washing the dishes to answering the phone to peeling an orange, he reminds us that each moment holds within it an opportunity to work toward greater self-understanding and peacefulness. This gift edition features Thich Nhat Hanh’s inspiring calligraphy, photographs from his travels around the world, and a revised afterword.

**Discover the transformative power of mindfulness with "Get Some Headspace," an essential guide for anyone seeking a calmer, more focused, and happier life.** This acclaimed book, by Andy Puddicombe, co-founder of the renowned Headspace app, offers a refreshingly accessible approach to meditation, demystifying ancient practices for the modern, busy individual.

Puddicombe's mission is simple yet profound: to encourage people to dedicate just ten minutes each day to the practice of being present. Within this concise timeframe, you can learn highly effective techniques designed to address common modern-day stressors. Whether you're feeling overwhelmed by the pressures of daily life, grappling with difficult emotions, or simply seeking to enhance your concentration, "Get Some Headspace" provides practical tools.

The book promises a journey towards significant improvements in well-being. By incorporating these simple meditation techniques into your routine, you can learn to quiet an anxious mind, navigate challenging circumstances with greater ease, and sharpen your focus. Furthermore, the benefits extend to improved sleep quality and the attainment of new levels of inner calm and personal fulfillment.

The scientifically recognized advantages of mindfulness and meditation are brought to life in this guide, thoughtfully adapted for those with limited time. Originally published as "Get Some Headspace," this reissue underscores the profound, life-altering impact that a mere ten minutes of daily meditation can yield. As Emma Watson aptly describes it, "It's kind of genius," and Bill Gates expresses his gratitude, stating, "I'm grateful to Andy for helping me on this journey."

Name: The Science of Meditation
Description:
Drawing on cutting-edge research, friends and Harvard collaborators Daniel Goleman and Richard Davidson expertly reveal what we can learn from a one-of-a-kind data pool that includes world-class meditators. They share for the first time remarkable findings that show how meditation - without drugs or high expense - can cultivate qualities such as selflessness, equanimity, love and compassion, and redesign our neural circuitry. Demonstrating two master thinkers at work, The Science of Meditation explains precisely how mind training benefits us. More than daily doses or sheer hours, we need smart practice, including crucial ingredients such as targeted feedback from a master teacher and a more spacious worldview. These two bestselling authors sweep away the misconceptions around these practices and show how smart practice can change our personal traits and even our genome for the better. Gripping in its storytelling and based on a lifetime of thought and action, this is one of those rare books that has the power to change us at the deepest level.

This therapist guide offers a comprehensive, evidence-based group program designed to foster resilience and recovery for individuals who have experienced trauma. Developed over twenty years, the Inner Resources for Stress (IR) program integrates mindfulness, mantra repetition, and other meditative techniques into a structured yet adaptable nine-session framework.

The IR approach is informed by developmental psychology and cultural responsiveness, drawing on cognitive-behavioral theories of trauma. This guide provides therapists with essential tools, including assessment guidelines, detailed session plans, and scripts for guided meditation practices. It also features a reproducible Participant Guide, allowing clients to engage with the material independently. Purchasers gain access to a companion website for downloadable printable materials and audio recordings of the guided meditations, enhancing the program's accessibility and effectiveness.

## Building a Thriving Workplace: A Manager's Guide to Mental Wellbeing

Creating a workplace where employees feel mentally supported is crucial for business success. It directly impacts how productive, innovative, and profitable a company can be. However, many managers lack the practical skills to build this kind of environment. Often, efforts are limited to meeting legal requirements, which misses the key psychological elements that truly enhance performance and can even create new problems.

The "Mental Wealth" framework, developed by Peter Diaz and Emi Golding from the Workplace Mental Health Institute, provides a strong, proactive way forward. This complete model helps managers and leaders create a mentally strong workplace by going beyond just following rules. It gives them the resources to actively help employees, challenge common misunderstandings about mental health, understand why certain issues arise, and see how employee wellbeing significantly affects the business. By concentrating on these psychological aspects, companies can develop a resilient, high-achieving team and gain the significant advantages of a modern approach to mental health. The framework outlines seven key areas for a psychologically safe workplace and offers practical steps, supported by real examples, for quick implementation and clear progress.

**Navigating Mental Health Challenges: A Guide for Families**

Millions of families face the challenge of supporting a loved one with a mental health condition. With over fifty million people diagnosed annually, mental illness affects individuals across all demographics, leaving no one untouched. When left untreated, these conditions can profoundly impact families and communities. Often, family members are the first to notice a problem, but knowing how to help or where to seek support can be overwhelming. The mental health system can feel complex and difficult to navigate.

This guide offers practical advice for understanding and addressing mental health concerns. It addresses crucial questions such as identifying the signs of mental illness, determining the best initial steps to take, and finding appropriate care. The book provides comprehensive information on various conditions, from depression, bipolar disorder, and anxiety to eating disorders, trauma-related disorders, and schizophrenia.

Readers will gain insights into helpful medications, understanding potential risks, and navigating privacy laws to discuss treatment with healthcare providers. It helps distinguish between typical adolescent struggles and potential illnesses. Through real-life scenarios and expert advice, presented in a compassionate and accessible manner, families will find clear guidance. Checklists for doctor's appointments are included to empower you to ask the right questions. This resource offers hope and a clear path forward for those feeling lost in the challenges of mental health care. Dr. Lloyd Sederer, a leading voice in mental health services, shares his extensive expertise to help families understand the signs of mental illness and explore treatment options, enabling you to approach healthcare professionals with confidence and clarity.

Name: Mental Health A Pocket Guide
Description:
- Increased consumer focus - New chapter on trauma-informed practice and care - New focus on loss and grief (including loss of employment, relationships, COVID and climate-related events) - Increased focus on self-care, self-awareness and support strategies - Greater emphasis on cultural awareness and working across the lifespan

## Telepsychiatry: A Practical Guide for Modern Mental Healthcare

*Telepsychiatry and Health Technologies: A Guide for Mental Health Professionals* offers a patient-centered approach to mental healthcare delivered through technology. This evidence-based guide moves beyond purely technical health informatics to focus on the practical application of technology within mental health workflows. It's designed for psychiatrists, psychologists, and other clinicians looking to understand the various modes, models, and methods of telepsychiatry.

The book features contributions from over 30 telepsychiatry practitioners across core mental health disciplines. It is rich with real-world clinical examples and hands-on guidance, illustrating effective strategies and their practical implementation. The editors aim to equip clinicians with the knowledge to leverage technology for efficient and effective population-level mental healthcare.

Structured to meet clinician needs, the book prioritizes practical application. Each chapter was comprehensively outlined before being assigned to contributors with specific clinical and therapeutic expertise, ensuring a logical flow and minimizing redundancy. *Telepsychiatry and Health Technologies* provides essential "how-to" guidance for establishing new telepsychiatry practices or integrating existing technologies. It covers vital topics like data collection, security protocols, and the use of electronic health records.

The guide explores technologies used in psychiatric assessment and treatment, including telephony, smartphones, mobile applications, email, secure texting, and videoconferencing. Over 30 case examples, featuring diverse patients and programs, demonstrate the effective management of various clinical techniques and populations through in-person, online, or hybrid models. Each chapter concludes with key learning objectives, making the book a foundational resource for training and an indispensable reference for professionals incorporating new technologies into their practice.

## Fostering a Mentally Healthy Workplace: Essential Strategies for Success

Creating a workplace that prioritizes mental health and wellbeing is no longer optional; it's a critical imperative. With statistics revealing that one in four individuals will face a mental health challenge, employers are increasingly called upon to cultivate supportive environments. This guide, recognized with a ***HIGHLY COMMENDED - HR & MANAGEMENT - BUSINESS BOOK AWARDS 2021***, offers expert insights for both leaders and employees, providing practical strategies to navigate the complexities of mental health at work.

The book emphasizes the profound impact of mental wellbeing on individual employees and overall business success. It explores how a mentally healthy workforce directly correlates with increased productivity, enhanced engagement, and a stronger organizational culture. Beyond understanding *why* it matters, the guide offers actionable steps for proactive wellbeing promotion. This includes developing a comprehensive "wellbeing strategy" and adapting to evolving workplace dynamics to optimize employee support. Crucially, it addresses the need to shift both employer and employee perspectives on mental health, fostering an open and understanding atmosphere. For those supporting staff experiencing mental ill health, the book provides practical guidance on managing challenges, addressing common stressors like anxiety, and offers real-world case studies with diverse solutions. It also highlights essential resources for individuals seeking further assistance. This resource is invaluable for anyone committed to nurturing a thriving, mentally healthy workplace.

Name: Mental Health, Naturally
Description:
With up-to-date research, illustrative examples, and a practical approach forindividuals and families, this handbook features an overview of mental healthdisorders, basic strategies for improving as well as preventing mental healthissues, and more.

Name: Student Mental Health
Description:
The chapter authors address life transitions and the university student experience, as well as the challenges of caring for university students with mental health issues. The book has positive strategies, including ways to foster mental health for distinct university student populations.

Name: Authoritative Guide to Self-help Resources in Mental Health
Description:
Self-help materials have become a prime source of psychological advice for millions of Americans. While many self-help resources provide high-quality information and support, others may be misleading, inaccurate, or even harmful. This indispensable volume reveals which are the good ones, which are the bad ones, and why. Based on the results of 5 national studies involving over 2,500 mental health professionals, the book reviews and rates 600+ self-help books, autobiographies, and popular films. In addition, hundreds of helpful Internet sites are described and evaluated by a clinical psychologist, and valuable listings are provided of national and online support groups. The concluding chapter presents practical guidelines for selecting an effective self-help resource. Addressing 28 of the most prevalent clinical disorders and life challenges--from ADHD, Alzheimer's, and anxiety disorders, to marital problems and mood disorders, to weight management and women's issues--this timely book will be tremendously useful to consumers and professionals alike.

Name: Understanding Mental Illness
Description:
“Skillfully crafted, thoughtful, and expertly written.” —Sheryl Denise Jones, MD “Comprehensive and educational . . . from a practical and relatable point of view." —Napoleon Higgins, MD “A well needed resource! . . . It allows us to better understand and support the people we care about, but who struggle with mental illness.” —Thomas Kerrihard, MD Get the straight facts about mental illness from two Harvard trained psychiatrists. More than 40 million people in the US suffer from mental health problems—yet less than half receive adequate care and treatment. Even in the 21st century with the most advanced medical care in the world, social stigma still surrounds psychiatric problems, and this, combined with a lack of understanding, perpetuates a national mental health crisis affecting those in need and their families. Ignoring and/or being unaware of a problem can have devastating effects in our families and for society at large—many people living with mental illness go untreated, and as a result, people with untreated mental illnesses make up one third of the nation’s homeless population and can be imprisoned. To meet these challenges, Dr. Carlin Barnes and Dr. Marketa Wills have written this necessary and comprehensive, practical guide to educate and help everyone better understand mental health. Each chapter offers insights and wisdom concerning a variety of psychiatric conditions, including: Mood disorders Anxiety disorders Personality disorders Substance abuse issues Eating disorders Women’s mental health issues Suicide in America Geriatric mental health Professional athletes and mental health And more Armed with this knowledge, you and your loved one can better appreciate the real struggles at hand, and as a result, seek the proper care needed.

## Understanding Mental Wellness in Adults with Down Syndrome

This comprehensive guide, **MENTAL WELLNESS IN ADULTS WITH DOWN SYNDROME**, offers an accessible yet in-depth look at the mental health of adults with Down syndrome. Drawing on the extensive experience of its authors, who co-founded the first medical clinic dedicated to adults with Down syndrome, the book addresses the wide range of psychosocial issues and mental disorders that can affect this population. It serves as an essential resource for parents, healthcare professionals, and caregivers supporting teens and adults with Down syndrome.

The core message of **MENTAL WELLNESS** is that fostering good mental health hinges on understanding and valuing both the strengths and challenges inherent in Down syndrome. The book helps readers differentiate between genuine mental health concerns and common characteristics of Down syndrome, such as unique coping mechanisms. For instance, while talking to oneself can sometimes indicate psychosis, many adults with Down syndrome utilize self-talk as a practical problem-solving technique.

### Key Updates and Expanded Topics in the Second Edition

The second edition features significant updates and new content to reflect the latest research and understanding:

*   **New Chapters:** Includes dedicated chapters on sensory issues, authored by Dr. Katie Frank, and regression.
*   **Expanded Focus:** Chapters on communication, concrete thinking, and visual memory have been expanded and are now presented separately for greater clarity.
*   **Updated Alzheimer's Research:** The chapter on Alzheimer's disease has been thoroughly revised, incorporating abundant new research findings.

### Exploring Specific Mental Health Conditions

**MENTAL WELLNESS** delves into a variety of conditions, offering insights into assessment and treatment options:

*   **Defining Normality:** What Is Normal?
*   **Self-Perception:** Self-Esteem & Self-Image
*   **Communication Styles:** Self-Talk
*   **Cognitive Flexibility:** Grooves & Flexibility
*   **Life Stages:** Life-Span Issues
*   **Social Interaction:** Social Skills
*   **Emotional Well-being:** Mood & Anxiety Disorders
*   **Repetitive Behaviors:** Obsessive-Compulsive Disorder
*   **Psychotic Disorders**
*   **Eating Challenges:** Eating Refusal
*   **Behavioral Issues:** Challenging Behavior
*   **Self-Harm:** Self-Injurious Behavior
*   **Autism Spectrum Disorder**
*   **Movement Disorders:** Tics, Tourette Syndrome & Stereotypies

While mental health problems are not a certainty for individuals with Down syndrome, certain biological factors and environmental stressors can increase susceptibility. This guide provides detailed assessment and treatment strategies for each condition, empowering caregivers to promote positive mental health and effectively manage challenging issues.

Name: A Planet for Rent
Description:
The most successful and controversial Cuban Science Fiction writer of all time, Yoss (aka José Miguel Sánchez Gómez) is known for his acerbic portraits of the island under Communism. In his bestselling A Planet for Rent, Yoss pays homage to Ray Bradbury’s The Martian Chronicles and 334 by Thomas M. Disch. A critique of Cuba in the nineties, after the fall of the Soviet Union and the dissolution of the Warsaw Pact, A Planet for Rent marks the debut in English of an astonishingly brave and imaginative Latin American voice. Praise for Yoss “One of the most prestigious science fiction authors of the island.” —On Cuba Magazine "A gifted and daring writer." —David Iaconangelo "José Miguel Sánchez [Yoss] is Cuba’s most decorated science fiction author, who has cultivated the most prestige for this genre in the mainstream, and the only person of all the Island’s residents who lives by his pen.” —Cuenta Regresiva Born José Miguel Sánchez Gómez, Yoss assumed his pen name in 1988, when he won the Premio David Award in the science fiction category for Timshel. Together with his peculiar pseudonym, the author's aesthetic of an impentinent rocker has allowed him to stand out amongst his fellow Cuban writers. Earning a degree in Biology in 1991, he went on to graduate from the first ever course on Narrative Techniques at the Onelio Jorge Cardoso Center of Literary Training, in the year 1999. Today, Yoss writes both realistic and science fiction works. Alongside these novels, the author produces essays, Praise for, and compilations, and actively promotes the Cuban science fiction literary workshops, Espiral and Espacio Abierto. When he isn’t translating, David Frye teaches Latin American culture and society at the University of Michigan. Translations include First New Chronicle and Good Government by Guaman Poma de Ayala (Peru, 1615); The Mangy Parrot by José Joaquín Fernandez de Lizardi (Mexico, 1816), for which he received a National Endowment for the Arts Fellowship; Writing across Cultures: Narrative Transculturation in Latin America by Ángel Rama (Uruguay, 1982), and several Cuban and Spanish novels and poems.

## A Fire Upon the Deep: A Journey Through the Zones of Thought

Vernor Vinge's Hugo Award-winning novel, *A Fire Upon the Deep*, now features a new introduction for the Tor Essentials line, poised to introduce a new generation of science fiction enthusiasts to Vinge's acclaimed body of work. As David Brin aptly states, "Vinge is one of the best visionary writers of SF today."

### The Universe of the Zones of Thought

The narrative unfolds thousands of years in the future, within a universe where the very potential of a mind is dictated by its spatial location. This cosmic partitioning creates distinct "regions of thought." At the apex are the Transcend, home to superintelligent entities. In stark contrast lie the Unthinking Depths, where only rudimentary creatures and basic technology can operate. The origin of these regions remains a profound mystery, a cosmic enigma that shapes the lives and capabilities of all sentient beings.

### A Galactic Catastrophe Unleashed

The fragile balance of this universe is shattered when the warring Straumli realm employs an ancient Transcendent artifact as a weapon. This act, however, unleashes an unimaginable power, resulting in the destruction of thousands of worlds and the enslavement of all natural and artificial intelligence. This cataclysmic event casts a long shadow over the galaxy, threatening all life.

### A Desperate Escape and Unexpected Captivity

Fleeing this encroaching galactic menace, Ravna crash-lands on an unfamiliar world. Their only companions are a ship-hold filled with children, preserved in cryogenic sleep, the sole survivors of a devastated space laboratory. Their precarious situation worsens as they fall into the hands of the Tines, an alien species with a formidable and harsh medieval culture. Ravna and the children become unwilling pawns in a brutal and unforgiving power struggle, their fate hanging precariously in the balance.

### Other Works by Vernor Vinge

*   **Zones of Thought Series:**
    *   A Fire Upon The Deep
    *   A Deepness In The Sky
    *   The Children of The Sky
*   **Realtime/Bobble Series:**
    *   The Peace War
    *   Marooned in Realtime
*   **Other Novels:**
    *   The Witling
    *   Tatja Grimm's World
    *   Rainbows End
*   **Collections:**
    *   Collected Stories of Vernor Vinge
    *   True Names

Please note: At the Publisher's request, this title is being sold without Digital Rights Management Software (DRM) applied.

Eversion is a remarkable and original Gothic science fiction novel. The story follows a well-funded and organized expedition of intrepid explorers on a quest for a remote and mysterious artifact. Their journey takes them north of Bergen aboard the schooner Demeter, seeking a narrow inlet that promises to lead to a vast, uncharted lake and their ultimate goal.

However, disaster looms. Doctor Silas Coade awakens from unsettling dreams aboard the steamship Demeter, finding himself in pursuit of an extraordinary find. This artifact is secreted within a lagoon in the icy inlets of Patagonia, its nature almost too incredible and strange to comprehend. As they approach their prize, Coade and the crew realize they are not the first to venture this far. A wreck lies ahead, and the force that destroyed it may pose a threat to them as well.

Shaking off his lingering nightmares, Doctor Silas Coade joins his fellow explorers on the deck of the zeppelin Demeter. He quickly understands that their mission has already taken a dangerously wrong turn. If any of them are to survive, Coade will have to take charge of the exploration and their very lives.

Name: Macroscope
Description:
Throughout history, man has been searching for better ways to gather information about his universe. But although they may have longed for it, not even the most brilliant minds could conceive of a device as infinitely powerful or as immeasurably precise as the Macroscope, until the twenty-first century. This is a story of mans desperate search for a compromise between his mind and his heart, between knowledge and humanity.

## The Last Human: A Galaxy-Spanning Debut

In a universe teeming with unfathomable alien intelligences, Sarya carries the weight of being humanity's last hope. This ambitious debut novel plunges readers into a galaxy-spanning adventure where the fate of a lost species rests on the shoulders of a single individual.

### A Hidden Existence

Most days, Sarya doesn't feel like the most terrifying creature in the galaxy. Her primary concerns revolve around maintaining her hidden identity among the diverse alien species populating Watertower Station. She also has to manage her adoptive mother's volatile temper, which occasionally leads to neighborly disputes. For a long time, Sarya accepted that she might never uncover the truth behind humanity's demise—why their species was deemed too dangerous to exist, or if she truly was the sole survivor of a civilization destroyed a thousand years prior.

### A Shattered Reality

Her quiet existence is irrevocably altered by a violent encounter with a bounty hunter and a massive kinetic projectile. This event shatters her life and forces a radical shift in her perspective. Now, at the helm of a stolen ship, Sarya embarks on a perilous journey. She's accompanied by an unlikely crew: a rebellious spacesuit, an android with a penchant for death and a history spanning sixty lifetimes, and a ball of fluff possessing an intellect in the thousands.

### Uncovering an Impossible Truth

Together, this peculiar group begins to unravel a truth that defies comprehension. Sarya starts to question if humanity's extinction and her own existence are merely pawns in a twisted cosmic game orchestrated by vast alien intellects. Even more astonishing, these powerful, enigmatic beings might be extending an invitation to Sarya—a chance to join their ranks and potentially offer humanity a second chance.

### A Masterful Debut

"The Last Human" is a brilliantly conceived and delightfully eccentric space opera. It masterfully explores themes of perspective, intelligence, and free will, all while delivering a thrilling and unforgettable journey through a strange and vibrant galaxy. As Andy Weir, author of "The Martian," aptly describes it, this novel is "a good old-fashioned space opera in a thoroughly fresh package." Dennis E. Taylor, author of "We Are Legion," praises its "big ideas and believable science amid a roller-coaster ride of aliens, AI, superintelligence, and the future of humanity."

Name: Ancillary Justice
Description:
Winner of the Hugo, Nebula, and Arthur C. Clarke Awards: This record-breaking novel follows a warship trapped in a human body on a quest for revenge. A must read for fans of Ursula K. Le Guin and James S. A. Corey. "There are few who write science fiction like Ann Leckie can. There are few who ever could." -- John Scalzi On a remote, icy planet, the soldier known as Breq is drawing closer to completing her quest. Once, she was the Justice of Toren -- a colossal starship with an artificial intelligence linking thousands of soldiers in the service of the Radch, the empire that conquered the galaxy. Now, an act of treachery has ripped it all away, leaving her with one fragile human body, unanswered questions, and a burning desire for vengeance.

## Adrian Tchaikovsky's "Shards of Earth": A New Space Opera Unveiled

Adrian Tchaikovsky, the celebrated author behind the Arthur C. Clarke Award-winning *Children of Time*, launches his new space opera, *Shards of Earth*. This first installment of *The Final Architecture* trilogy plunges readers into a far-future adventure, lauded by Christopher Paolini as "one of the most interesting and accomplished writers in speculative fiction."

### The Architects' Lingering Shadow

Eighty years have passed since Earth's catastrophic destruction by an enigmatic alien race known as the Architects. Humanity survived, but the war left deep scars and necessitated the development of individuals like Idris, telepathically attuned to the Architects. However, their subsequent, unexplained disappearance rendered these enhanced humans obsolete. Idris, a freelance salvage operator who has not aged since his augmentation, now drifts through the galaxy, seeking anonymity. His solitary existence is disrupted when he and his crew discover a mysterious, abandoned object in the void – an object unmistakably crafted by the Architects. This discovery raises a critical question: are their ancient enemies returning, and if so, why?

### A Galaxy-Spanning Race for Survival

Possessing an artifact of immense value and a secret many are willing to kill for, Idris and his crew find themselves relentlessly pursued. They face threats from ruthless gangsters, zealous cults, and vigilant governments. Their journey becomes a desperate race across the cosmos, not merely an escape from danger, but a perilous quest for answers that could shape the future of humanity.

### Critical Acclaim for "Shards of Earth"

The novel has been met with widespread praise:

*   "Enthralling, epic, immersive and hugely intelligent" – Stephen Baxter.
*   "He writes incredibly enjoyable sci-fi, full of life and ideas" – Patrick Ness.
*   "Thoughtful, sweeping space adventure" – *SFX*.
*   "Brilliant science fiction" – James McAvoy.

The Metagalactic Grand Prix is a thrilling, high-stakes cosmic competition that unites the galaxy's many sentient races. This grand tradition, born from the ashes of the devastating Sentience Wars, now stands as a potent symbol of peace and interspecies understanding. It artfully combines the excitement of gladiatorial combat with the charm of beauty pageants and the spectacle of concert extravaganzas, all while subtly keeping alive the historical rivalries between civilizations.

Every cycle, civilizations from across the cosmos gather to witness and compete in the Grand Prix, a magnificent display of diverse talents. Species showcase their unique abilities through song, dance, or any performance their distinct physiology allows. This demonstrates incredible creativity, even for those without vocal cords, limbs, or facial features. For any new species that has recently achieved sentience, the Grand Prix offers a crucial chance to gain recognition from the galaxy's established powers. Participation is mandatory, and the penalty for failing to impress the judges is severe: the complete extermination of their species. Humanity, having just discovered the vastness of the universe, expected grand tales of diplomacy and war. Instead, they found a realm defined by dazzling shows, electrifying music, and pure performance. Earth's future, it seems, will be secured not by warfare, but by song. Our planet's hopes now lie with Decibel Jones and the Absolute Zeroes, whose performance must captivate the galaxy to ensure Earth's survival.

Name: Elemental Haiku
Description:
A fascinating little illustrated series of 118 haiku about the Periodic Table of Elements, one for each element, plus a closing haiku for element 119 (not yet synthesized). Originally appearing in Science magazine, this gifty collection of haiku inspired by the periodic table of elements features all-new poems paired with original and imaginative line illustrations drawn from the natural world. Packed with wit, whimsy, and real science cred, each haiku celebrates the cosmic poetry behind each element, while accompanying notes reveal the fascinating facts that inform it. Award-winning poet Mary Soon Lee's haiku encompass astronomy, biology, chemistry, history, and physics, such as "Nickel, Ni: Forged in fusion's fire,/flung out from supernovae./Demoted to coins." Line by line, Elemental Haiku makes the mysteries of the universe's elements accessible to all.

Name: Purpose of Flowers
Description:
Purpose of Flowers is a Haiku book of eighty poems with Illustrations. These poems evoke the spirit of oneness with nature and delve into the meaning and purpose of life. Haiku poetry is an ancient form of lyrics that trace their origins as early as the seventh century. A blend of Modern, Contemporary and Classic this book is divided into three parts that take the readers on a journey of minimalistic form of art poetry. The three lines in seventeen syllables use natural imagery to focus on a brief moment in time and draw inspirations from ordinary observations. A perfect gift for your special someone and to yourself.

Name: Book of Haikus
Description:
A compact collection of more than 500 poems from Jack Kerouac that reveal a lesser known but important side of his literary legacy “Above all, a haiku must be very simple and free of all poetic trickery and make a little picture and yet be as airy and graceful as a Vivaldi pastorella.”—Jack Kerouac Renowned for his groundbreaking Beat Generation novel On the Road, Jack Kerouac was also a master of the haiku, the three-line, seventeen-syllable Japanese poetic form. Following the tradition of Basho, Buson, Shiki, Issa, and other poets, Kerouac experimented with this centuries-old genre, taking it beyond strict syllable counts into what he believed was the form’s essence. He incorporated his “American” haiku in novels and in his correspondence, notebooks, journals, sketchbooks, and recordings. In Book of Haikus, Kerouac scholar Regina Weinreich has supplemented a core haiku manuscript from Kerouac’s archives with a generous selection of the rest of his haiku, from both published and unpublished sources.

Name: The World of Haiku
Description:
A collection of original haiku poetry and Japanese art. Sumi-E pen and ink brush work. Haiku is an ancient form of writing poetry from Japan, and is very strict in its structure. Three sections of three lines each. The first and third lines must be five syllables. The second line must be seven syllables. Haiku usually refers to nature somewhere in the poetry. This book includes all original Haiku by a living poet. There has been a huge revival of interest in this form of poetry and its beauty is recognized by the modern world.

Name: Morning Haiku
Description:
Poems of commemoration and loss for readers of all ages, from a leading writer of the Black Arts Movement and the American Poetry Society's 2018 Wallace Stevens Award–winner. Sonia Sanchez's collection of haiku celebrates the gifts of life and mourns the deaths of revered African American figures in the worlds of music, literature, art, and activism. In her verses, we hear the sounds of Max Roach "exploding in the universe," the "blue hallelujahs" of the Philadelphia Murals, and the voice of Odetta "thundering out of the earth." Sanchez sings the praises of contemporaries whose poetic alchemy turns "words into gems": Maya Angelou, Richard Long, and Toni Morrison. And she pays homage to peace workers and civil rights activists from Rosa Parks and Congresswoman Shirley Chisholm to Brother Damu, founder of the National Black Environmental Justice Network. Often arranged in strings of twelve or more, the haiku flow one into the other in a steady song of commemoration. Sometimes deceptively simple, her lyrics hold a very powerful load of emotion and meaning. There are intimate verses here for family and friends, verses of profound loss and silence, of courage and resilience. Sanchez is innovative, composing haiku in new forms, including a section of moving two-line poems that reflect on the long wake of 9/11. In a brief and personal opening essay, the poet explains her deep appreciation for haiku as an art form. With its touching portraits and by turns uplifting and heartbreaking lyrics, Morning Haiku contains some of Sanchez's freshest, most poignant work.

Name: Basho
Description:
Matsuo Basho stands today as Japan's most renowned writer, and one of the most revered. Yet despite his stature, Basho's complete haiku have never been collected under one cover. Until now. To render the writer's full body of work in English, Jane Reichhold, an American haiku poet and translator, dedicated over ten years to the present compilation. In Barbo: The Complete Haiku she accomplishes the feat with distinction. Dividing the poet's creative output into seven periods of development, Reichhold frames each period with a decisive biographical sketch of the poet's travels, creative influences, and personal triumphs and defeats. Supplementary material includes two hundred pages of scrupulously researched notes, which also contain a literal translation of the poem, the original Japanese, and a Romanized reading. A glossary, chronology, index of first lines, and explanation of Basho's haiku techniques provide additional background information. Finally in the spirit of Basho, elegant semi-e ink drawings by well-known Japanese artist Shiro Tsujimura front each chapter.

Name: Write Your Own Haiku for Kids
Description:
This book gives a bit of history as well as what a haiku is and then gives the seven steps to writing a haiku. […] Have you ever written a haiku? They are fun to write and read! Be sure to check out this fun book to get you started. -- Crafty Moms Share blog

Name: Haiku
Description:
A poetry collection honoring the haiku—complete with poet biographies, translator commentary, and Japanese artwork This celebration of what is perhaps the most influential of all poetic forms takes haiku back to its Japanese roots. Beginning with poems by the seventeenth and eighteenth-century masters Basho, Busson, and Issa, the anthology goes all the way up to the late twentieth century to provide a survey of haiku through the centuries, in all its minimalist glory. The translators have balanced faithfulness to the Japanese with an appreciation of the unique spirit of each poem to create English versions that evoke the joy and wonder of the originals with the same astonishing economy of language. An introduction by the translators and short biographies of the poets are included. Reproductions of woodblock prints and paintings accompany the poems.

Name: The Penguin Book of Haiku
Description:
'A revelation' Sunday Times, Books of the Year 2018 The first Penguin anthology of Japanese haiku, in vivid new translations by Adam L. Kern. Now a global poetry, the haiku was originally a Japanese verse form that flourished from the sixteenth to nineteenth centuries. Although renowned for its brevity, usually running three lines long in seventeen syllables, and by its use of natural imagery to make Zen-like observations about reality, in fact the haiku is much more: it can be erotic, funny, crude and mischievous. Presenting over a thousand exemplars in vivid and engaging translations, this anthology offers an illuminating introduction to this widely celebrated, if misunderstood, art form. Adam L. Kern's new translations are accompanied here by the original Japanese and short commentaries on the poems, as well as an introduction and illustrations from the period.

Name: Missing Person
Description:
From acclaimed thriller writer Sarah Lotz, hailed by Stephen King as "vastly entertaining," a new novel about a group of amateur detectives infiltrated by the sadistic killer whose crimes they're investigating. Reclusive bookseller Shaun Ryan has always believed that his uncle Teddy died in a car accident twenty years ago. Then he learns the truth: Teddy fled his home in Catholic, deeply conservative County Wicklow, Ireland, for New York and hasn't been heard from since. None of Shaun's relatives will reveal why they lied about his uncle's death or why they want Shaun to leave the whole affair alone. But Shaun has a burning need to find out the truth. His search is unsuccessful until he's contacted by Chris Guzman, a woman who runs a website dedicated to matching missing-persons cases with unidentified bodies. Chris and her team of cold-case obsessives suspect that Shaun is looking for the "Boy in the Dress," one victim in a series of gay men murdered by the same killer. But who are these internet fanatics really, and how do they know so much about a case that has stumped police for decades? Soon armchair sleuths and professional investigators are on a collision course with a sadistic serial killer who's gotten away with his crimes for far too long - and now they're in his sights.

Name: Missing Persons
Description:
A missing person is an individual whose whereabouts are unknown and where there is some concern for his or her wellbeing. In the UK, around 250,000 people are reported missing every year, with the majority being children under the age of 18. Despite the fact that missing persons are a social phenomenon which encompasses vast areas of interest, relatively little is known about those who go missing, what happens to them while they are missing, and what can be done to prevent these incidents from occurring. This groundbreaking book brings together for the first time ideas and expertise across this vast subject area into one interconnected publication. It explores the subjects of missing children, missing adults, the investigative process of missing person cases, and the families of missing persons. Those with no prior knowledge or professionals with focused knowledge in some areas will be able to expand their understanding of a variety of topics relevant to this field through detailed chapters which advance our understanding of this complex phenomenon, discuss what is unknown, and suggest the best and most important steps forward to further advance our knowledge.

Name: Missing 411- Hunters
Description:
(www.canammissing.com- missing person site)Author David Paulides has released the sixth installment in his best selling series, Missing 411. The books have revealed the names and facts behind people who have disappeared in the national parks and forests of the world. The identification of over 59 geographical clusters of missing people in North America is one of the mysterious, unsettling and unexplained elements in the Missing 411 series. Missing 411- Hunters explains a subset of the research and documents 148 cases of hunters who have vanished in four countries. The incidents parallel other disappearances documented in prior Missing 411 books. The vast majority of the cases in this edition are new and they don't appear in other books in the series. The mystery and stories of the victims will baffle and confound the avid outdoorsman and seasoned hunter.Countries Included:United States- 26 StatesCanada- 9 ProvincesAustraliaAzerbaijianDisappearances Documented:148348 PagesOther Books in the Series:Missing 411- Western United StatesMissing 411- Eastern United StatesMissing 411- North America and BeyondMissing 411- The Devil's in the DetailMissing 411- A Sobering Coincidencewww.canammissing.com

## Unexplained Vanishings: Cases That Defy Logic

The world is filled with mysteries, and perhaps none are as unsettling as the cases of people who disappear without a trace, leaving behind only questions. While we often believe that science and technology can explain every phenomenon, certain disappearances continue to baffle investigators and captivate the public imagination. These are not ordinary missing person cases; they are instances where individuals seem to have vanished into thin air, defying all logical explanations.

### When the Ordinary Becomes Extraordinary

From individuals who have walked into dense forests and never emerged, to those who have disappeared from seemingly secure locations without any sign of forced entry, the spectrum of unexplained vanishings is vast and perplexing. Even in the age of ubiquitous surveillance, some people have seemingly dematerialized directly in front of security cameras. Despite our desire for simple, mundane answers, a significant number of these cases remain profoundly mysterious, challenging our understanding of reality.

This book delves into some of the most perplexing and mind-boggling instances of abrupt vanishing ever recorded. We explore:

*   **The Ill-Fated Alien Abductee:** A case that suggests otherworldly involvement.
*   **Anjikuni, the Vanishing Village:** A community that seemingly ceased to exist.
*   **Abducted by Bigfoot:** Encounters with the legendary creature and its potential role in disappearances.
*   **The Fierce Legend of Ambrose Bierce:** The enigmatic fate of the renowned author.
*   **What Happened to Gerry Irwin?:** A puzzling disappearance that continues to haunt investigators.
*   **The Mysterious Disappearance of a Manic Street Preacher:** A case shrouded in intrigue.
*   **The Troubling Case of Brandon Lawson:** A modern enigma that defies easy answers.

And many more chilling accounts that will leave you questioning the limits of the known.

Scroll back up and order your copy today to explore these captivating and inexplicable mysteries!

Name: Missing Person Case Files Solved
Description:
Vanished into thin air... Disturbing cases of people gone missing and being found again. Read about their mysterious and sometime chilling journey.Missing people are far more common than you might think. Every year, around the world, hundreds of thousands of people vanish into thin air. Usually, they have simply been involved in a freak accident that has led to their death. Sometimes, they vanish on purpose and don't wish to be found. On very rare occasions, there is violence at play and the person has been deliberately removed from the public or even killed.In this book, we will be examining the various circumstances in which people happen to vanish. These circumstances can vary from elegant plans of their own design to freak accidents that result in memory loss. In some cases, we will find that there is a malicious party at play and a far greater crime to be solved. But in every single case, we will learn of someone who vanishes before being found again.So, read on to discover the strange and mysterious events which can drive people away, as well as the emotions and motivations that lead them to come home. With so many people disappearing across the planet, the stories of those who are able to come home are inspirational, fascinating, and - in some cases - almost unbelievable. In this book, we will learn the true stories of the lost and found, the real life events which see people arrive back home after most people might have given up hope. Read on and discover just what it takes to come back.Here are some of the cases that you will find inside:* The young boy abducted by a crazed man* The boy whose kidnap inspired a search and rescue team* The controversial lawyer who vanished for eight years* The rapper who dropped off the radar* The actress who vanished without warning* The man kidnapped by North Korea* The man struck down by a little known medical issue* And many more!Scroll back up and order your copy today

**Cathy Moulton: A Cold Case That Still Haunts Portland**

The disappearance of a child is a parent's deepest fear, a void that can consume a family. For the loved ones of 16-year-old Cathy Marie Moulton, this nightmare became a stark reality on September 24, 1971. A seemingly ordinary day in Portland, Maine, was irrevocably altered when Cathy vanished without a trace. This event, marked by a sudden and unexplained departure, left a profound and lasting impact on her family and community.

**An Enduring Mystery: Case #71-60141**

Over four decades later, Portland Police Department case #71-60141, designated "Cathy Moulton Missing-Endangered," stands as one of the longest-running active missing person cases in North America.
